HTML:
Build and evolve the core framework behind quantitative decision-making.


We are looking for a Quantitative Developer to join our Quantitative Research team at InCommodities Asset Management. In this role, you’ll build and evolve the core simulation framework used for pricing, valuation, and risk analysis across the company.


From day one, you’ll create the abstractions, tooling, and workflows that make quantitative decision-making scalable, reproducible, and trusted - while also supporting the modelling of structured power products in close collaboration with stakeholders. Sounds like your kind of challenge? Then keep reading.


The Journey

At InCommodities Asset Management, we're not just building a company – we're facilitating the future of renewable energy.

Back in 2022, we launched our dedicated Asset Management (AM) team in Aarhus, Denmark, with a clear ambition: to accelerate the green transition by making renewable energy investments both attractive and bankable. Through Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs), we help renewable asset owners and producers unlock value, manage risk, and ensure stable, long-term returns.


In 2025, we took the next bold step – becoming an independent company. This move gives us full autonomy and a sharp commercial focus on asset trading and optimization.

Now, we’re a fast-growing team of 35+ ambitious professionals, and we’re just getting started. With big plans to scale across the entire renewable value chain, now is the perfect time to come on board and be part of something meaningful – and truly exciting.


Ready to rethink the future of energy with us?


What you’ll do

You’ll help build the technical foundation that enables quantitative decision-making across the company. Your core responsibility is to design, build, and evolve our simulation and research framework, ensuring that models and flows are scalable and reproducible across the organization.

This involves ongoing improvements in the engineering maturity of the quant platform by setting standards and practices that keep the codebase robust, maintainable, and production ready. Day-to-day, you’ll:


  • Build and maintain the core backend for simulation, back testing, and forward-looking market and cashflow analysis
  • Design and implement a lean, modular framework architecture that balances performance, flexibility, and maintainability as usage grows
  • Create reusable abstractions and tooling that accelerate research and reduce friction between ideas and implementation
  • Establish and uphold engineering standards across the quant codebase (testing, CI/CD, structure, documentation, versioning)
  • Ensure correctness and reproducibility through structured workflows, automated testing, and clear assumptions
  • Drive modelling and forecasting of structured power products in close collaboration with the Portfolio Management team and other stakeholders


What you bring

  • A master’s or PhD in a quantitative field (e.g. Mathematics, Engineering, Computer Science, or Data Science)
  • Relevant professional experience from a financial company (e.g. bank, fund/asset-manager, trading company) and/or a strong academic background with hands-on technical projects
  • Strong skills in Python and experience with SQL
  • Solid understanding of software engineering fundamentals such as design patterns, API/library design, testing, and CI/CD
  • Preferably an interest in numerical methods, optimization, dynamic programming, and asset pricing
  • You’re fluent in English, both written and verbal


How you work

  • You bring strong commercial drive and are motivated by delivering solid results
  • You’re structured and systematic with a strong sense of correctness and quality
  • You are accountable and thrive by taking ownership of several areas of work
  • You enjoy building robust systems and are comfortable working independently on complex problems that others depend on
  • You’re people-oriented and collaborate naturally with both quant and commercial stakeholders
  • You bring a can-do attitude and thrive in a fast-paced, evolving environment


What you can expect

  • A tech-driven scale-up company, which challenges how energy assets are traded and optimized
  • A great start-up environment with a high degree of autonomy and flexibility to drive real impact
  • Curious, ambitious and bold people who come together to rethink what’s possible
  • A unique company culture where we live up to our values: Honesty, Transparency, and Rethink
  • Flat hierarchy — no dress code, no meaningless bureaucracy


Like what you’re reading? Apply now – we’d love to hear from you!

Title: Product Manager, Data Insights
Description:
About Us

nShift is the leading global provider of cloud delivery management solutions (SaaS), we enable the frictionless shipment and return of almost one billion shipments across 190 countries each year. We are headquartered in London and Oslo and have over 500 employees across offices in Sweden, Finland, Norway, Denmark, the United Kingdom, Poland, the Netherlands, Belgium, and Romania.

Our software is used by many of the world leading e-commerce, retail, manufacturing, and 3PL shippers due to us having over 1000 carriers integrated into our platform, nearly 3 times more than our competitors!

If you buy goods online, there is a strong chance that nShift has powered that delivery, so come and join us as we shape the future of shipping, one frictionless journey at a time.

About You

You think like a product manager and work like a data scientist. You have shipped ML-powered products to real customers and can point to the decisions you made, not just the ones made around you. Messy data, ambiguous model outputs, and stakeholders who don't yet see why the data matters are familiar territory.

You think in terms of compounding: how every data point collected today improves predictions tomorrow, and how that improvement builds a position competitors cannot close. Data insights is nShift's path from a tool company to an intelligence platform, and that scope motivates you rather than overwhelming you.

You are as comfortable in a Jupyter notebook as in a leadership meeting, and you know when each is needed.

Purpose of role

The mission is to define what nShift builds with that data, validate it before engineering commits, and ship intelligence products that help shippers make better decisions. A shipper asking "how can I improve my delivery experience" should get an answer powered by the full network, not just their own history. Done well, that becomes a compounding intelligence layer: every data point collected today improves suggestions tomorrow, and the improvement creates a position no competitor can close.

The intelligence layer draws from two sources. Structured operational data, such as shipment telemetry and carrier performance, feeds statistical models and predictions. Qualitative data, such as customer support interactions, delivery exception messages, and community feedback, carries signal that numbers alone cannot surface. LLMs make that signal extractable and usable at scale. This role owns both.

The role sits at the intersection of product management and data science. You have the technical depth to build ML proof-of-concepts independently and the commercial judgment to translate those capabilities into products customers pay for. You write the strategy and the first notebook.

What You'll Be Doing

Define and own the data driven product strategy


Own the roadmap for nShift's data intelligence layer: carrier performance prediction, cross-customer benchmarking, ML-driven carrier recommendations, and related capabilities.
Translate nShift's data asset into a sequenced product strategy that delivers near-term value while building toward the compounding intelligence layer vision.
Define the information architecture for the data insights layer: what data is collected, how it is normalized (in partnership with adjacent teams), and how it is exposed to upstream products.
Make hard prioritization calls: what to build first to validate the ML hypothesis, what to defer, and what to kill when the data doesn't support the bet.
Own the qualitative intelligence pipeline alongside the quantitative one: define which unstructured sources carry decision-relevant signal (support tickets, delivery exception messages, customer forum, carrier communications), how LLM-extracted insights are structured for downstream use, and how qualitative and quantitative signals combine into a coherent intelligence product.
Own the positioning and differentiation narrative: why this dataset is uniquely nShift's, why no competitor can replicate it, and how to articulate that defensibility to enterprise buyers.



Build and validate before engineering commits


Run ML proof-of-concepts independently: data exploration, feature engineering, model selection, and initial validation, using Python, notebooks, and the data available in nShift's data layer.
Define the evaluation framework for both ML models and LLM pipelines. For ML features: what accuracy thresholds matter, how to measure model performance in production, how to distinguish signal from noise. For LLM-based features: how to assess output consistency, factual grounding, and quality at scale, where traditional accuracy metrics don't apply.
Design lightweight experiments that answer the key questions before a full engineering team is committed. Does the prediction accuracy justify the product? Does cross-customer data improve the model significantly? Is the data we have sufficient, or do we need to instrument more?
Translate POC findings into clear build, kill, or iterate decisions backed by data.



Partner across data, engineering, and commercial teams


Work closely with engineers to define the data models, pipelines, and normalization standards needed to power ML features. You are a customer and co-designer of the data layer, not a passenger.
Drive integration of data insights capabilities into existing products. Work closly with your PM peers to make this happen.
Engage directly with customers and prospects across the full spectrum, from small shippers to global enterprises, to validate intelligence use cases: what decisions do they make today that better data would improve? What would they pay for? What do they trust?
Build the commercial narrative: ROI frameworks, value quantification, and positioning for the target buyer.



Govern the data responsibly


Define the anonymization, aggregation, and consent frameworks that make cross-customer benchmarking legally and ethically sound, and credible to enterprise customers who will ask hard questions.
Work with Legal and the DPO to ensure data usage within the intelligence layer meets GDPR and contractual obligations. The data asset is only valuable if customers trust nShift to use it appropriately.
Establish transparency standards for ML-powered recommendations: when does a merchant see why a carrier was recommended? What level of explainability builds trust without revealing proprietary network data?



What You'll Bring

Must-have


Hands-on data proficiency across both paradigms: you can build and validate ML proof-of-concepts (data exploration, feature engineering, model training, evaluation) using Python and standard ML tooling (scikit-learn, pandas, XGBoost, or equivalent), and you can design and evaluate LLM-based analysis pipelines (prompt design, retrieval-augmented approaches, output quality evaluation). You are not a data scientist or an ML engineer, but you can work like one when the problem requires it.
Shipped ML-powered products: you have personally driven an ML or data intelligence product from initial hypothesis to production, including defining evaluation criteria, partnering with data engineers and scientists, and making commercial decisions based on model performance data.
PM fundamentals with commercial depth: 3+ years shipping B2B SaaS products. You can build a business case, define pricing, run customer discovery, and hold direct conversations with enterprise buyers about ROI.
Data literacy that earns engineering respect: you understand data models, pipeline design, normalization challenges, and the practical constraints of real production data. You can review a data schema and immediately ask the right questions.
Clear communication across the technical and commercial boundary: you can explain a precision-recall tradeoff to a CPO and a carrier event data use case to a data engineer in the same afternoon, without condescending to either.
Excellent written and verbal English: you will write strategy documents, ML experiment briefs, customer-facing product narratives, and present to leadership, often in the same week.



Nice to have


Experience with time-series or logistics domain data: carrier performance modelling, routing optimization, ETA prediction, or related domains.
Familiarity with data platform concepts: data lake houses, feature stores, model registries, observability for ML in production.
Background in cross-customer benchmarking or network intelligence products, where the value comes from aggregation rather than individual customer data.
Experience with GDPR-compliant data product design in a multi-tenant B2B SaaS context.
Nordic or European logistics market experience: carrier landscape, regulatory environment, freight economics.
Track record of building the first data product at a company, defining standards rather than inheriting them.



Please ensure you upload your CV in English

At nShift we believe in embracing diversity in all forms and fostering an inclusive environment for everyone which we believe is essential for our continued success. We're an equal-opportunity employer which means that all applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, family or parental status, national origin, veteran, neurodiversity, or disability status.

Title: Lead Analytics Platform Developer
Place: Gentofte, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join us in this role where you’ll be the Business Application Responsible for the Analytics Workspace, a JupyterHub-based platform built on cloud Kubernetes and used across Trading & Revenue. You’ll co-develop the platform with Commercial Digital Technology, guide and test deliveries, and make sure the platform evolves in line with user needs across analytics, algo development, and model development.

Welcome to Analytics

You’ll be part of Data Science & Platforms, one of three teams in Trading & Revenue’s Analytics function, where you, together with your colleagues, will deliver the platforms and products behind trading and revenue decisions. You’ll work closely with quantitative developers, analysts, model developers, and Commercial Digital Technology to shape a reliable, scalable platform that supports both current users and new analytics capabilities. As a team, we value flexibility, experimentation, knowledge sharing, and collaboration. We care about building an inclusive environment where different perspectives help us create better solutions.

You’ll play an important role in:


owning the Analytics Workspace on the Trading & Revenue side, ensuring it remains reliable, relevant, and well aligned with business needs
co-developing platform capabilities with Commercial Digital Technology through coding, prototyping, reviewing pull requests, and defining acceptance tests
enabling strong platform foundations for analytics, algo development, and machine learning use cases, including compute, environments, data access, deployment, logging, and monitoring
supporting users through onboarding, day-to-day platform support, and close collaboration with stakeholders across Trading & Revenue
maintaining best practices, tools, templates, and ways of working for analytics engineering and the wider analytics community
driving the platform roadmap, including improvements, migrations, and adjacent capabilities such as machine learning, streaming and AI tooling.


To succeed in the role, you:


have strong Python development skills and apply sound engineering practices across testing, packaging, and CI/CD
bring hands-on experience with JupyterHub-based platforms, Kubernetes, Airflow and cloud infrastructure in Azure, AWS, or similar environments
work confidently across DevOps or MLOps topics and can contribute to platform design, delivery, and operational excellence
collaborate well with internal users and external engineering partners, and communicate clearly with both technical and business stakeholders
take ownership, build trust, and stay close to user needs while helping specialist teams contribute and co-own solutions
are motivated to grow your understanding of Trading & Revenue analytics, and you bring curiosity, structure, and a practical mindset to complex platform challenges.


Maybe you’ve read the above and can see you have some transferable skills, even though they don’t quite match all the points. If you think you can bring something to the team, we still encourage you to apply.

Shape the future with us

Send your application to us as soon as possible. We’ll be conducting interviews on a continuous basis and reserve the right to take down the advert when we’ve found the right candidate.

As an applicant or employee, you may request reasonable work and position accommodation or adjustments via accommodation@orsted.com.

Please note that for your application to be taken into consideration, you must submit your application via our online career pages and answer the screening questions relevant for your country. We don't take applications or inquiries from external recruiters or agencies into account for this position.

Position salary range:

For this position, the expected base pay range is kr58,000.00- kr87,000.00 for the advertised location. The offered base salary will be determined within this range based on relevant skills, experience, and expected role fulfilment within the stated pay range to ensure a fair and competitive offer. Where applicable, you may also be eligible for variable payment and a comprehensive benefits package.

Our benefits package:

For this position, employees who meet eligibility requirements have access to: employer‑paid pension (11% contribution), full insurance coverage (accident, life, critical illness, and health insurance); five weeks of paid holiday plus 10 additional paid days off per holiday year; a 3% holiday allowance; access to Ørsted Life & Fitness Vitality including fitness classes, physiotherapy, massage, and wellbeing programmes; a multimedia package (mobile phone, PC, internet); staff club membership with sport and cultural activities; and access to the company canteen (monthly lunch scheme or daily tickets).

Pre-employment background check:

Certain positions may require a background check as part of the hiring process. If applicable, you will be informed during the recruitment process. Background checks are conducted by an external provider in accordance with local legal requirements.

Title: AI Udvikler
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Bliv AI-udvikler hos KOMBIT og vær med til at udvikle fremtidens AI-løsninger til kommunerne!




Har du lyst til at udvikle ansvarlige, sikre og værdiskabende AI-løsninger – og arbejde med de nyeste værktøjer i et stærkt fagligt miljø? Så har vi netop den rette mulighed for dig.




Et stærkt it-fællesskab

I KOMBIT har de danske kommuner et fælles omdrejningspunkt med stærke og specialiserede kompetencer på digitaliseringsområdet. Vi er et kommunalt ejet it-fællesskab, der er ansvarlig for at indkøbe, forvalte, drifte og videreudvikle tidssvarende og stabile it-løsninger og -infrastruktur til gavn for borgere og virksomheder. Sammen med kommunerne og it-leverandørerne arbejder vi for, at borgere og virksomheder i Danmark får en bedre service og billigere og mere sammenhængende it-løsninger. KOMBITs ambition er at sikre fortsat kommunal digital bevægelse - i dag og i fremtiden.




Vi har et stærkt fokus på at levere ansvarlige og sikre AI-løsninger, der kan komme i produktion og skabe reel værdi for kommunerne.




Dine primære ansvarsområder

Du bliver tilknyttet specialet Design & Arkitektur og en del af KOMBITs fokuserede AI core team. Her arbejder vi sammen med bl.a. KL, kommunerne, leverandører samt interne og eksterne eksperter om at udvikle, modne og udbrede AI-løsninger i den kommunale sektor.

Vi søger udviklere, der vil være med til at bygge konkrete AI-løsninger fra idé til produktion. Det kan være, at du har en baggrund inden for data science, erfaring som full stack-udvikler eller stærke kompetencer inden for DevOps, CI/CD og moderne udviklingsmiljøer. Det vigtigste er, at du har lyst til at arbejde praktisk og nysgerrigt med AI-teknologi, softwareudvikling og løsninger, der kan skabe reel værdi for kommunerne.




Som AI-udvikler hos KOMBIT kommer du bl.a. til at arbejde med udvikling, test, integration, drift og videreudvikling af AI-løsninger. Vi anvender AI aktivt i alle faser af udviklingen – fra analyse, design og kodning til test, dokumentation, kvalitetssikring og drift. Du vil derfor også få mulighed for at arbejde med AI-agenter og andre nye værktøjer, der kan styrke både udviklingsprocessen og de løsninger, vi leverer.




Du kan eksempelvis blive involveret i arbejdet med vores AI Assistent, som hjælper kommunale sagsbehandlere med at anvende fagsystemer og søge information i lovgivningen, og som også anvendes internt i KOMBIT. Derudover kan du bidrage til udvikling af nye AI-produkter, fælles komponenter og platforme, screening af nye use cases, dataanalyse, evaluering, overvågning og sikker drift af AI-løsninger.




Vi er på udkig efter flere nye kollegaer til teamet, så uanset om du primært ser dig selv som data scientist, full stack-udvikler, DevOps-profil eller AI-nysgerrig softwareudvikler, hører vi meget gerne fra dig.




Fokus på dig og dit (arbejds)liv

Vi tilbyder dig et job, hvor omdrejningspunktet er samarbejde på tværs, og hvor du vil arbejde med nogle af Danmarks største it-løsninger. Samtidig bliver du en del af en ambitiøs virksomhed, der står i udviklingens tegn, både hvad angår de mange eksisterende løsninger samt forventningen om en fremtid med endnu bredere portefølje inden for digitalisering og it-dreven innovation i kommunerne.




I KOMBIT er det vigtigt, at alle medarbejdere finder en god balance mellem arbejde og privatliv. Vi skaber motiverende rammer, der giver medarbejderne frihed til at planlægge og løse deres opgaver ud fra individuelle behov. Af samme grund vil du hurtigt opleve, at der er højt til loftet og rig mulighed for at sætte dit præg i en hver fyldt med spændende opgaver. Vi har som organisation fokus på dig og dit (arbejds)liv i balance – vi kalder det LiFE!

Lyder det som noget for dig?




Så tag første skridt mod en spændende karriere i frontlinjen for digital udvikling, og send os dit CV senest d. 2. august 2026. Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at sende en mail til Chef for Design & Arkitektur, Lars Vraa (LVR@kombit.dk).




Vi screener relevante kandidater, hvor vi spørger ind til motivationen for at søge stillingen samt forventninger til vilkår for at sikre, at vi finder den bedst egnede kandidat til stillingen.




Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig.

Bliv AI-udvikler hos KOMBIT og vær med til at udvikle fremtidens AI-løsninger til kommunerne!


Har du lyst til at udvikle ansvarlige, sikre og værdiskabende AI-løsninger – og arbejde med de nyeste værktøjer i et stærkt fagligt miljø? Så har vi netop den rette mulighed for dig.


Et stærkt it-fællesskab

I KOMBIT har de danske kommuner et fælles omdrejningspunkt med stærke og specialiserede kompetencer på digitaliseringsområdet. Vi er et kommunalt ejet it-fællesskab, der er ansvarlig for at indkøbe, forvalte, drifte og videreudvikle tidssvarende og stabile it-løsninger og -infrastruktur til gavn for borgere og virksomheder. Sammen med kommunerne og it-leverandørerne arbejder vi for, at borgere og virksomheder i Danmark får en bedre service og billigere og mere sammenhængende it-løsninger. KOMBITs ambition er at sikre fortsat kommunal digital bevægelse - i dag og i fremtiden.


Vi har et stærkt fokus på at levere ansvarlige og sikre AI-løsninger, der kan komme i produktion og skabe reel værdi for kommunerne.


Dine primære ansvarsområder

Du bliver tilknyttet specialet Design & Arkitektur og en del af KOMBITs fokuserede AI core team. Her arbejder vi sammen med bl.a. KL, kommunerne, leverandører samt interne og eksterne eksperter om at udvikle, modne og udbrede AI-løsninger i den kommunale sektor.

Vi søger udviklere, der vil være med til at bygge konkrete AI-løsninger fra idé til produktion. Det kan være, at du har en baggrund inden for data science, erfaring som full stack-udvikler eller stærke kompetencer inden for DevOps, CI/CD og moderne udviklingsmiljøer. Det vigtigste er, at du har lyst til at arbejde praktisk og nysgerrigt med AI-teknologi, softwareudvikling og løsninger, der kan skabe reel værdi for kommunerne.


Som AI-udvikler hos KOMBIT kommer du bl.a. til at arbejde med udvikling, test, integration, drift og videreudvikling af AI-løsninger. Vi anvender AI aktivt i alle faser af udviklingen – fra analyse, design og kodning til test, dokumentation, kvalitetssikring og drift. Du vil derfor også få mulighed for at arbejde med AI-agenter og andre nye værktøjer, der kan styrke både udviklingsprocessen og de løsninger, vi leverer.


Du kan eksempelvis blive involveret i arbejdet med vores AI Assistent, som hjælper kommunale sagsbehandlere med at anvende fagsystemer og søge information i lovgivningen, og som også anvendes internt i KOMBIT. Derudover kan du bidrage til udvikling af nye AI-produkter, fælles komponenter og platforme, screening af nye use cases, dataanalyse, evaluering, overvågning og sikker drift af AI-løsninger.


Vi er på udkig efter flere nye kollegaer til teamet, så uanset om du primært ser dig selv som data scientist, full stack-udvikler, DevOps-profil eller AI-nysgerrig softwareudvikler, hører vi meget gerne fra dig.


Fokus på dig og dit (arbejds)liv

Vi tilbyder dig et job, hvor omdrejningspunktet er samarbejde på tværs, og hvor du vil arbejde med nogle af Danmarks største it-løsninger. Samtidig bliver du en del af en ambitiøs virksomhed, der står i udviklingens tegn, både hvad angår de mange eksisterende løsninger samt forventningen om en fremtid med endnu bredere portefølje inden for digitalisering og it-dreven innovation i kommunerne.


I KOMBIT er det vigtigt, at alle medarbejdere finder en god balance mellem arbejde og privatliv. Vi skaber motiverende rammer, der giver medarbejderne frihed til at planlægge og løse deres opgaver ud fra individuelle behov. Af samme grund vil du hurtigt opleve, at der er højt til loftet og rig mulighed for at sætte dit præg i en hver fyldt med spændende opgaver. Vi har som organisation fokus på dig og dit (arbejds)liv i balance – vi kalder det LiFE!

Lyder det som noget for dig?


Så tag første skridt mod en spændende karriere i frontlinjen for digital udvikling, og send os dit CV senest d. 2. august 2026. Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at sende en mail til Chef for Design & Arkitektur, Lars Vraa (LVR@kombit.dk).


Vi screener relevante kandidater, hvor vi spørger ind til motivationen for at søge stillingen samt forventninger til vilkår for at sikre, at vi finder den bedst egnede kandidat til stillingen.


Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig.

Title: Product Analytics Engineer
Place: Aarhus Municipality, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
The role

We are looking for a Product Analytics Engineer to join our product team in Aarhus.

This is not a role where you only build pipelines. You own Candeno's data and analytics layer within your domain: the data warehouse, the BI and dashboards, and the instrumentation that turns product usage and AI interactions into reliable data. Part of the work is figuring out the right question before you build the answer. The rest is building it well.




We work AI-augmented. Modern AI tools are a natural part of how we work, so more of the routine building is handled for you. That makes the harder, more valuable work the judgement: what to measure, how to model it, and whether the numbers can be trusted. We use AI to move faster, and we believe people are what make sure we build the right things and trust the right data.




Your work will reach hundreds of thousands of people across the Nordics: job seekers navigating labour market reforms, employment professionals adapting to new digitalisation strategies, and the institutions managing that change. The dashboards and data you build inform decisions inside Candeno and in the hands of our customers, so keeping them trustworthy matters.




What you will work on

Candeno serves municipalities, unemployment funds, and educational institutions across Denmark, Norway, Sweden, and Finland. The platform has three sides: candidates navigating the labour market, advisors guiding them through digitalisation and compliance workflows, and companies recruiting talent. One platform, many contexts. Our products increasingly run on data and AI, and this role builds the foundation underneath them.




Discovery (analysis and design)

•    Work with product people and customers to understand what a dashboard or a data model actually needs to answer, before you build it. AI helps us move fast, so the harder work is knowing what is worth measuring.

•    Model the data. Decide access patterns and weigh the tradeoffs between flexibility and control, with architecture direction from our Lead Architect.




Delivery (build and operate)

•    Build and maintain the data warehouse, data pipelines, and ETL or ELT flows between Candeno's platform and the warehouse.

•    Build and maintain internal and customer-facing dashboards, so the right data reaches the right audience in a form that drives decisions.

•    Instrument our AI chats, agents, and product usage so requests and usage signals become structured, first-class data, and work with engineers to keep it accessible for AI and ML use.

•    Write clean, maintainable code, using AI tools where they help and applying judgement to what they produce.




Quality and craft

•    Keep data trustworthy. Care about data quality and make it something the team can rely on.

•    Meet regulatory requirements in a data-sensitive sector, with guidance from our legal advisor.

•    Share what you know and help raise data competence across the team. This is knowledge-sharing, not people management.




Who we are looking for

You are a data and analytics engineer who is as comfortable with the question as with the query. Your foundation is solid delivery in data and BI, and your edge is knowing what is worth measuring before you build it.




You will need:

•    A few years building and maintaining data warehouses and BI or dashboards, ideally in SaaS or a product company. Full build experience matters more than years.

•    Solid data modelling skills, and comfort navigating the tradeoffs between flexibility and control.

•    Experience building data pipelines and ETL or ELT flows. Modern transformation tooling such as dbt is a plus.

•    An understanding of what AI and ML need from data, in structure and quality. You can grow into this.

•    A considered approach to AI in your workflow. You use modern tools well, and you check what they produce critically.

•    Clear communication. You can explain a data tradeoff to a product manager, an engineer, and a customer, and make it feel simple.

•    Comfort working with customers directly.




The kind of person who does well here:

•    Curious about the question, not only the query.

•    Ownership mindset. You follow a problem all the way through.

•    Comfortable making decisions without a perfect foundation, and honest about the tradeoffs when you do.

•    Direct. You share work early, give feedback clearly, and push back when something is not right.




What the first six months look like

After one month

You understand Candeno's platform, our BI setup and data flows, and the team. You have taken ownership of your first scoped tasks and you are active in how the team works.




After three months

You are building key parts of the warehouse and BI independently and contributing to product and AI instrumentation. You are starting to engage with customer analytics needs, with support.




After six months

You reliably own and deliver within the data and analytics domain. The dashboards and instrumentation you build are in real use, and you are a dependable go-to for product analytics questions, working within architecture direction from the Lead Architect.




Working at Candeno

We are a scale-up. That means real ownership, real impact, and a product that is still being shaped by the people who work on it.

We are based in Aarhus, and this is an on-site role. The team is small, cross-functional, and works closely together. You will have real ownership of your work and close proximity to product, engineering, and our customers.

We are direct and focused on doing the right thing for users. We share work early and expect you to do the same.




What we offer

•    Pension scheme and health insurance

•    5 extra days off after 2 years of seniority (10 in total)

•    Daily lunch from Meyers Madhus

•    A shared milestone programme that celebrates successes big and small

•    An active social life: team events, clubs, and an engaged social committee




The process

•    A short screening call

•    A first interview, a deeper conversation about your experience and the role

•    A case interview, a practical brief sent in advance

No cover letter. You will answer three short questions when you apply.




About Candeno

Candeno is a Nordic employment technology company based in Aarhus. We build the platform that connects candidates, advisors, and companies across municipalities, unemployment funds, and educational institutions in Denmark, Norway, Sweden, and Finland. Our work sits at the intersection of public sector compliance and modern product thinking, and we take both seriously.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer (Audio)
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Location: Copenhagen




Type: Contract or Full-time




About Us

We are a small, senior team working on a technology-driven project in an international context, focused on building autonomous distributed intelligent physical systems. As we continue to grow, we are expanding our engineering organization to strengthen our capabilities and accelerate product development.




What You’ll Build

You will sit at the intersection of multi-modal data processing and Edge computing in our Audio team building the software pipelines and machine learning models that transform streams of audio and time/frequency data into real-time intelligence on embedded platforms.




Key Responsibilities

ML Model Development with a focus on Audio: Design, train, and iterate on efficient ML architectures for real-time acoustic detection and classification real-world signals.
Audio DSP Pipeline Engineering: Implement and optimize pre-processing techniques (e.g., beamforming, noise reduction, filtering, spectral analysis) to condition audio data before it hits the ML model.
Edge Deployment & Optimization: Deploy ML models onto edge hardware, aggressively optimizing for latency, memory footprint, and power consumption using techniques like quantization, pruning, and distillation.
End-to-End MLOps: Build automated pipelines 
Real-World Validation: Drive rigorous field testing and experiments to benchmark model accuracy, SNR, and detection range against key performance indicators.




Required Skills and Experience

Research & Publication: Demonstrated academic rigor with a strong publication record in audio/acoustic ML.
Industry/Domain Expertise: Meaningful experience applying ML/DSP in relevant industrial domains.
Product-Minded Engineering: Proven ability to bridge the gap between research prototypes and scalable, end-to-end product systems.
Strategic & Systems Thinking: A high-level view on architecting for long-term scalability; able to articulate technical trade-offs clearly.
High-Level Communication: Ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear business value for stakeholders.
Core Technical Proficiency: Strong proficiency in Python (PyTorch, NumPy) and Audio DSP (e.g., spatial audio/beamforming) along with familiarity with MLOps practices.




Nice to Have

Experience with real-time streaming inference frameworks.
Familiarity with the constraints of audio acquisition hardware (e.g., microphone array geometries).
Experience with C/C++ for embedded audio processing.
Edge Deployment & Optimization: Proven track record of deploying ML models on edge platforms using runtimes like TFLite, TensorRT, or ONNX Runtime.

Title: Data Science - Manager - Retail
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

As a Manager for Data Science consulting, you operate at the intersection of business strategy, data, and emerging AI technologies. You work closely with clients to shape their data and AI agendas—translating business challenges into actionable opportunities, advising on the right approaches, and guiding initiatives from idea through to value realization.

You bring a strong technical foundation, but your primary focus is on understanding client needs, framing problems, and designing impactful solutions. You are a trusted advisor who can connect strategy, data, technology, and organizational change—while staying hands-on enough to explore data, prototype solutions, and leverage modern AI tools.

Qualifications


Strong experience in data, AI, or analytics roles, with exposure to both technical implementation and business-facing work
Proven ability to engage clients, structure problems, and translate business needs into data/AI solutions
Proven experience in developing and deploying AI models at scale, including natural language processing (NLP), computer vision, and reinforcement learning.
Expertise in machine learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, and scikit-learn, as well as experience with cloud-based AI platforms (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure).
Strong understanding of data engineering concepts, including data pipelines and big data technologies such as Spark, Hadoop, SQL, and NoSQL.
Experience working with modern AI ecosystems (e.g., cloud platforms, LLMs, AI tools, or data platforms)
Strong communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders at multiple levels
Comfortable working in ambiguous environments, exploring new problem spaces, and expanding into adjacent domains
Demonstrated curiosity and eagerness to learn and apply new technologies, especially within the rapidly evolving AI landscape
Experience in consulting, digital transformation, or cross-functional environments is a strong plus


Additional Information

Set Yourself Apart With


Experience defining AI or data strategies for organizations
Hands-on experience with modern AI tools (e.g., generative AI, copilots, automation platforms)
Exposure to data architecture, data engineering, or product development
Track record of driving AI adoption or innovation initiatives within organizations
Advanced degree in a relevant field (e.g., data science, engineering, business, or similar)


What We’re Looking For

We’re looking for someone who is:


Equally comfortable in the boardroom and with a dataset
A translator between business ambition and technical reality
Curious, adaptable, and excited about the fast-moving AI landscape
Motivated to go beyond pure data science into broader problem-solving and innovation


Company Description

Publicis Sapient is a digital transformation partner helping established organizations get to their future, digitally enabled state, both in the way they work and the way they serve their customers. We help unlock value through a start-up mindset and modern methods, fusing strategy, consulting, and customer experience with agile engineering and problem-solving creativity. United by our core values and our purpose of helping people thrive in the brave pursuit of the next, our 20,000+ people in 53 offices around the world combine experience across technology, data sciences, consulting, and customer obsession to accelerate our clients’ businesses through designing the products and services their customers truly value.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
DATA ENGINEER

Build the data and AI foundation behind real trading and risk decisions, on a genuinely modern data stack.




Who we are

Lind Capital is a proprietary trading company focused on the financial markets. We combine market expertise, cutting-edge technology, data-driven decisions, and quantitative models to execute the best possible trades.

We bring together talented people from diverse backgrounds in an informal work environment focused on creating results together. We empower our team to make decisions and prefer direct collaboration over bureaucracy.




Our diverse team of 25+ are based in Aarhus, Denmark.




The role

Data and AI are central to Lind Capital’s success and key to our future growth, and we are looking to add to our data team that currently consists of six Data and ML Engineers.

We run a modern data stack centered on Python (incl. Polars), Airflow, Parquet and Apache Iceberg, with Athena, Postgres, Kafka, and more in the mix. In this role, you’ll turn structured and unstructured data into trusted, well-modeled datasets that power production applications, analytics, and reporting — while helping us expand reliability, performance, and capabilities across our Data Lakehouse.




You won't just feed data to AI applications — you'll help build them. You'll work in a team that actively experiments with AI across both agentic engineering and agentic workflows — from AI-assisted development to the internal AI tooling we develop ourselves.




Key responsibilities

Collaborate closely with trading and risk professionals to define data needs and translate them into durable datasets and interfaces.
Design, build, and operate batch and streaming pipelines in our cloud-based Data Lakehouse.
Use AI-assisted development tools fluently and help build the data foundations and internal tooling that let AI and ML systems work reliably and safely against our data.
Implement data quality checks and improve pipeline observability (monitoring, alerting, and fast debugging when things break).




What we offer

A genuinely modern data stack where the data products and AI tools you build visibly drive live investment and risk decisions — real impact, at a firm small enough that it shows.
Close mentorship from six experienced Data and ML Engineers, with the support and code reviews to help you level up quickly.
A culture grounded in quantitative thinking, curiosity, and experimentation.
A dynamic environment that balances a disciplined approach to data pipelines with the freedom to innovate.
An inclusive, caring, and psychologically safe workplace where diverse perspectives, ideas, and approaches lead to high performance.




What we are looking for

Some hands-on experience with Data or Software Engineering — through work, internships, or your own projects. We care more about how you think than years on a CV.
Strong Python engineering skills (e.g. clean code, tests, packaging).
Solid SQL, and a working understanding of data stores for both transactional and analytical workloads.

HTML:
DATA ENGINEER

Build the data and AI foundation behind real trading and risk decisions, on a genuinely modern data stack.


Who we are

Lind Capital is a proprietary trading company focused on the financial markets. We combine market expertise, cutting-edge technology, data-driven decisions, and quantitative models to execute the best possible trades.

We bring together talented people from diverse backgrounds in an informal work environment focused on creating results together. We empower our team to make decisions and prefer direct collaboration over bureaucracy.


Our diverse team of 25+ are based in Aarhus, Denmark.


The role

Data and AI are central to Lind Capital’s success and key to our future growth, and we are looking to add to our data team that currently consists of six Data and ML Engineers.

We run a modern data stack centered on Python (incl. Polars), Airflow, Parquet and Apache Iceberg, with Athena, Postgres, Kafka, and more in the mix. In this role, you’ll turn structured and unstructured data into trusted, well-modeled datasets that power production applications, analytics, and reporting — while helping us expand reliability, performance, and capabilities across our Data Lakehouse.


You won't just feed data to AI applications — you'll help build them. You'll work in a team that actively experiments with AI across both agentic engineering and agentic workflows — from AI-assisted development to the internal AI tooling we develop ourselves.


Key responsibilities

  • Collaborate closely with trading and risk professionals to define data needs and translate them into durable datasets and interfaces.
  • Design, build, and operate batch and streaming pipelines in our cloud-based Data Lakehouse.
  • Use AI-assisted development tools fluently and help build the data foundations and internal tooling that let AI and ML systems work reliably and safely against our data.
  • Implement data quality checks and improve pipeline observability (monitoring, alerting, and fast debugging when things break).


What we offer

  • A genuinely modern data stack where the data products and AI tools you build visibly drive live investment and risk decisions — real impact, at a firm small enough that it shows.
  • Close mentorship from six experienced Data and ML Engineers, with the support and code reviews to help you level up quickly.
  • A culture grounded in quantitative thinking, curiosity, and experimentation.
  • A dynamic environment that balances a disciplined approach to data pipelines with the freedom to innovate.
  • An inclusive, caring, and psychologically safe workplace where diverse perspectives, ideas, and approaches lead to high performance.


What we are looking for

  • Some hands-on experience with Data or Software Engineering — through work, internships, or your own projects. We care more about how you think than years on a CV.
  • Strong Python engineering skills (e.g. clean code, tests, packaging).
  • Solid SQL, and a working understanding of data stores for both transactional and analytical workloads.
  • Comfort with Git and good engineering habits — testing, CI/CD, and code review.
  • Genuine interest in working with AI tools day-to-day — both using them to ship faster and thinking critically about where they help and where they don’t.
  • A strong communicator and team player who thrives in a cross-functional team.


We value potential over perfect match, and we encourage you to apply even if you don't meet all the above. We consider candidates across experience levels.


Location

We support flexible working arrangements; however, success in this role depends on close collaboration, which works best when colleagues are physically together. You should therefore expect to work most of the time from our Aarhus office. Our working language is English, and we welcome applications from non-Danish speakers.


Benefits

  • Professional development and clear career growth opportunities.
  • Competitive compensation and employee shares.
  • Good maternity/paternity terms.
  • Modern office environment with a healthy canteen and snacks.
  • Visa support (if needed).


The practical stuff

Start date: As soon as possible.


To apply, please submit your CV, grade transcript, cover letter, and other relevant documents via the link below.


Applications will be processed on a rolling basis.

Title: Data Engineer - Supply Chain Advanced Analytics - Viby
Description:
Are you excited by building scalable data solutions that directly impact Supply Chain decisions across a global organization? Curious about how data can truly drive business decisions - and not afraid to challenge the status quo to get there?

You will be part of a collaborative Advanced Analytics team where we co-create data-driven solutions with the business and continuously improve our platforms and way of working. We value curiosity, dialogue and the courage to challenge ideas to create better outcomes.

How You Will Make an Impact?

As part of the Supply Chain Advanced Analytics product team, you will work across a broad analytics landscape supporting functions such as SC Finance, Logistics, Planning, QEHS and Procurement.

You will contribute to building and maintaining robust data solutions while collaborating closely with business stakeholders to deliver real value. You will not only implement solutions but also seek to understand business challenges, ask the right questions and help continuously improve how data is used across the organization.

Your Key Responsibilities Will Include


Build and maintain end-to-end data pipelines (ingestion, transformation and delivery)
Implement enhancements to improve functionality and performance of supported solutions
Provide technical feedback and drive improvements to operational practices
Lead and coordinate UAT and PAT with business stakeholders, ensuring solutions are challenged, validated and aligned with real business needs
Share and document knowledge to support team development and future scalability


What Will Make You Successful?

We are looking for a data-focused IT Developer with strong analytical and collaboration skills, who thrives in a self-managing, collaborative and business-driven environment. You are curious about both data and the business behind it and motivated to understand how solutions create value. You have strong communication skills and can translate between technical and business perspectives as well as an eagerness to learn and continuously develop your technical and business skills. Further, we are looking for below professional competencies


Hands-on experience with Azure data services (e.g., Databricks, Data Lake, Synapse, Azure SQL, Data Factory, Azure Data Explorer)
Good understanding of Azure as a platform
Solid experience with data systems, analytics and databases
SQL proficiency and working knowledge of relational databases
Basic to intermediate Python knowledge
Familiarity with data modelling and data pipeline technologies
Experience with Git and branching strategies
Understanding of CI/CD pipelines and deployments
Experience working closely with business stakeholders (requirements gathering, alignment, etc.)


What Do We Offer?

You will join a team of 21 specialists working across Data Foundation, Power BI, SAP BW, SAP Analytics Cloud, BODS and Power Apps. Together, we deliver business-driven analytics solutions that strengthen decision-making across the Supply Chain.

We offer


A collaborative and diverse product team working closely with the business
Exposure to a wide range of technologies and data platforms
Opportunities to shape future analytics capabilities
A strong culture of knowledge sharing and continuous improvement
A direct impact on global Supply Chain transformation


Our team delivers solutions such as BW reports, Power BI dashboards, Power Apps, curated datasets and machine learning models - with AI agents as our next step. All solutions are built in close partnership with stakeholders, ensuring real business value.

Would you like to join us?

If you are passionate about data engineering and want to make a real impact in a global Supply Chain organization, we encourage you to apply.

Please apply as soon as possible, as we process applications on a continuous basis.

For more information, please contact Magda Merecka at magmg@arlafoods.com

Shape the Future of Dairy

Arla is a global leader in the dairy industry, committed to enabling good food choices that make life better, providing people with natural, sustainable nourishment, while taking care at every step to ensure Arla is a choice they can feel good about. If you are looking to shape the future with an ambitious global cooperative that truly cares about your growth, where everyone feels valued and empowered, and collaboration is the core of culture, Arla is a choice you can feel good about.

Title: Quant Infrastructure Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Imagine working with cutting-edge machine learning models, designing robust and scalable software infrastructure, and collaborating with dedicated colleagues – all while playing a key role in scaling our business even further.




Does this sound exciting? Then you might be our new Quant Infrastructure Engineer at Alipes.




At Alipes, we have been a market leader in automating financial markets since our inception in 2008, pioneering fully automated natural language processing systems to read and interpret financial news. To accomplish our goal of being the best at what we do, we are focused on building a world-class ML engineering workflow, ensuring seamless training, deployment and monitoring of our predictive models.




As a core member of the Quant team, your work will be instrumental in developing the infrastructure enabling us to transform massive unstructured financial data sets into production-ready inference models that drive real-time trading decisions. You will work with technologies such as Python, Kubernetes, Ray and Airflow. In doing so, you will get a chance to develop and maintain the infrastructure needed for scalable, high-performance ML systems, ensuring a seamless pipeline from dataset generation through model prototyping to production deployment.




Key responsibilities include

Designing, optimizing and maturing our infrastructure to support distributed model training and experiment tracking, as well as further developing internal Python tools for smooth interaction.
Building and automating robust data pipelines for dataset generation and feature engineering, while enabling data version control.
Developing software for rapid experimentation and deployment, including automated testing, continuous integration, and delivery.
Improve feature generation system- develop a feature store with definition and automated generation.
Transitioning to a more structured table based dataset store.
Implementing best practices in software development, ensuring maintainability, scalability, and efficiency of ML systems through automated testing and real-time monitoring.
Improving model deployment workflows, focusing on minimizing latency, ensuring reproducibility, and enabling fast iterations.




You will get instant validation of your work and experience short feedback cycles, where going from inception to deployment can be a matter of hours. There will be no red tape to cut and no sales people to consult.




About the team

All of this will happen in an informal atmosphere where technical discussions are valued, and you are encouraged to take ownership of, and pride in, your work. You will impact the direction of the Quant team, prioritize your work and choose the tools that get the job done.




We are a tight-knit modeling team with passionate quantitative researchers covering 9 nationalities – a mix of PhD’s and MSc’s with expertise in statistical analysis, mathematical modeling and machine learning. We enjoy collaboration and are always willing to lend a helping hand. We are working alongside two other teams of software developers and traders that implement and conceive the mathematical models together with us. What makes this constellation work great is that all of the individual team members write code and are willing to engage on a deep, technical level of understanding.




About you

A scientific and inquisitive mind.
Fluency with computer science fundamentals, specifically data structures and algorithms.
Experience working with ‘out-of-core’ datasets.
Experience with Python, in particular the data pre-processing, ETL pipelines and distributed systems.
Familiarity with strongly typed languages like C# or C/C++ or Rust.
A few years of experience in production-grade environments working with Machine Learning based applications.
Experience with distributed computing frameworks (for example Spark, Ray or Dask).
Familiarity with containerization technologies (for example Docker Compose or Kubernetes).




Nice to have

Experience working with the full Machine Learning stack and a strong MLOps foundation, going from dataset generation through model training and real-life validation and monitoring.
Experience working with tools like PyTorch, Tensorflow, XGBoost and/or Catboost.
Familiarity with algorithmic trading concepts.
PhD or MSc degree in engineering, computer science, mathematics or physics.




What we can offer you

Competitive benefits, including pension, health insurance, and 30 days of vacation.
A modern office in Copenhagen (Nordhavn) with a flexible hybrid work model.
A flat hierarchy with high levels of trust, autonomy, and freedom to make decisions.
A valued role with unique responsibilities and plenty of room for creativity.
Fast feedback and recognition of your contributions in a high-growth company.
Strong team spirit - successes are celebrated with company events and trips.




Practicalities

To apply, please send us your CV. If your profile looks like a good match, we will get in touch to coordinate an online technical test as a first step in the interview process.




If you have specific questions about the role, you are welcome to email us at quant_jobs@alipescapital.com. Please do not send in your applications to this email address, as our interview team will not have visibility to review.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Spirii develops world-class charging products and digital solutions for electric vehicles. Our Digital Development department fosters a listen, learn, and lead culture — allowing us to continuously improve how we work. We're looking for a hands-on Data Engineer to build and evolve Spirii's data platform and pipeline infrastructure. Working closely with our Data Lead and domain teams, you'll design and maintain reliable ELT pipelines, ensure data arrives in Amazon Redshift correctly and on time, and support the analytics layer that powers insights across EV charging events, subscriptions, and financial transactions. This is a unique opportunity to shape Spirii's data infrastructure from an early stage — establishing standards for ingestion, orchestration, and data reliability as we scale.

Your Responsibilities

Pipeline & Ingestion


Design, build, and maintain scalable ELT pipelines that bring data from product backends, third-party services, and operational systems into Amazon Redshift.
Own ingestion reliability: monitoring, alerting, and incident response for pipeline failures.
Evaluate and improve orchestration tooling and scheduling.


Data Platform & Infrastructure


Evolve Spirii's data platform architecture: storage, compute, access control, and cost optimisation in Redshift.
Implement and maintain infrastructure-as-code practices for data workflows (Terraform/CDK or similar).
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for data infrastructure and transformations.


Data Quality & Governance


Implement data quality checks, freshness monitoring, and anomaly detection across ingestion and transformation layers.
Work with Analytics Engineers and domain teams to define and enforce data contracts and schema standards.
Ensure GDPR-compliant data handling: PII identification, access control, and data retention policies.


Collaboration & Stakeholders


Partner with Engineering and Product teams to instrument events correctly at the source — schemas, identifiers, and change management.
Collaborate with Data team to ensure the analytics layer is built on reliable, well-modelled foundations.
Contribute to a strong data culture by creating and enhancing documentation, runbooks, data lineage, discoverability and incident response capabilities.


About You


You care deeply about data reliability and building pipelines that teams can depend on.
You're hands-on: you build, test, and ship pipelines and infrastructure improvements yourself.
You think of data pipelines as products — you care about uptime, observability, and the downstream consumers who depend on them.
You're pragmatic: you know when to engineer robustly and when to keep things lean.
You collaborate well across teams and communicate clearly about trade-offs and status.


Qualifications


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
3+ years of experience in data engineering or a related role with a strong data focus.
Strong SQL skills and solid experience building production data pipelines.
Hands-on experience with a modern data warehouse (Redshift preferred; BigQuery/Snowflake/Databricks also relevant).
Experience with orchestration tools (Apache Airflow or similar).
Proficiency in Python for data work (pipeline scripts, transformations, tooling).
Solid understanding of ELT/ETL concepts, including incremental loading, CDC, and idempotency.
Familiar with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, CDK, or similar).
Familiar with data governance concepts: access control, PII handling, GDPR-minded practices.


Nice to Have


Experience with data observability tooling.
Experience with event/data contract patterns in product analytics (schemas, versioning, instrumentation).
Experience with finance data and/or subscription/transaction domains.
Experience with dbt (understanding of transformation layer and how it interfaces with pipelines).
Machine learning infrastructure experience is a plus, but not required for this role.


Benefits

It is important for us to create a healthy, diverse, fun, and creative working environment, so besides all the basic benefits, we also focus a lot on social activities, flexible working hours and a good work-life balance.


Flexible Work Environment: Enjoy a collaborative, inclusive, and open workplace designed to fit your needs and support work-life balance.
Competitive Package: A competitive salary, an 11% company pension plan, and a comprehensive benefits package.
Time to Recharge: Benefit from 32 paid vacation days (5 weeks + 5 extra) and paid parental leave to focus on what matters most.
Career Growth: Access professional development opportunities and grow your career in a high-impact, high-growth industry.
Stay Energized: Enjoy excellent coffee, tea, free snacks, and subsidized lunches at the office.
Stay Active: Use our Spirii gym and shared company car to keep moving.
Make an Impact: Be part of a team driving the future of sustainable energy while working on meaningful projects.
Two innovation days per month for YOU to choose what you work on.


The position is a permanent position in Copenhagen, Denmark. Danish language is not a requirement, but fluent English language skills are required.

About Spirii – Who We Are

Spirii is a full-service e-Mobility provider, which offers hardware and software charging solutions to companies in the private and the public sector. Spirii has recently celebrated its six-year anniversary as a founded company; however the people in Spirii have extensive experience working with electric cars and charging, as well as the development of future transport and energy systems.

Spirii is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences. Spirii strives to create an inclusive and diverse workplace, and we encourage all qualified applicants to apply regardless of age, gender, race, sexual/religious orientation, or disability.

Title: AI Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
As our next AI Engineer, you will contribute to solving meaningful challenges across the life sciences industry. We offer specialised management consulting services, working with top-tier life sciences companies as well as biotech startups — locally and globally. From strategy development to hands-on implementation, we support our clients were impact matters most.







About the role




Generative and agentic AI has moved from demos to production. With this, one of our key focus areas is building products with an agentic AI engine at their core that are reliable, secure, auditable, and good enough to put in front of a medical writer or a regulatory reviewer.




That challenge has two halves:




The AI engine: Orchestration, retrieval, context engineering, tool use, guardrails, and the evals and observability that make an agentic system able to solve business-critical tasks in a regulated environment.
The product around it: The UI, APIs, services, data layers, integrations, and deployment that turn that engine into robust, secure, scalable software customers depend on.




Today these two halves often pull against each other. We are building a team where they pull together. As an AI Engineer, you will focus on the AI engine itself: designing, building, evaluating, and improving agentic systems that solve real business problems in highly regulated environments.

Your responsibilities include:




Design, build, and ship AI-powered tools that help life science teams create, review, and manage critical documents and data.
Build features such as grounded retrieval, summarisation, document comparison, question answering, and content classification with traceability back to source.
Design agentic workflows capable of handling complex business processes.
Build evaluation frameworks and observability capabilities that ensure reliability and quality.
Ensure solutions are secure, auditable, and scalable in regulated cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GCP).
Collaborate closely with clinical, regulatory, quality, and commercial experts to understand their workflows and implement AI solutions that create measurable value.




You bring:




Strong Python skills and solid software engineering fundamentals.
Hands-on experience with at least one major cloud (AWS, Azure, or GCP).
Deep, hands-on experience with enterprise LLMs and their APIs (e.g. Anthropic/Claude, OpenAI, Google), including tool/function calling, structured outputs, and the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
Experience building agentic systems with modern orchestration frameworks such as Pydantic AI, LangGraph, OpenAI Agents SDK, AutoGen / Microsoft Agent Framework, or LlamaIndex.
Strong command of retrieval techniques including RAG and graph-RAG, vector search, embeddings, reranking, and document-processing pipelines.
Practical context-engineering skills and the ability to consistently improve AI quality through prompt and context design.
Experience with evals and observability as first-class concerns, including evaluation suites, tracing, regression testing, reliability, latency, and cost management.
Familiarity with tooling such as LangSmith, Langfuse, Braintrust, Arize Phoenix, MLflow, DeepEval, or Promptfoo.
A solid foundation in data science fundamentals, data analysis, and statistical thinking.
Strong communication skills in English.




Nice to have




Understanding of pharmaceutical clinical and regulatory documents and workflows.
Awareness of GxP, 21 CFR Part 11, GDPR, HIPAA, and computer system validation.
Experience with semantic computing and document structuring.
Experience deploying secure, compliant applications in regulated cloud environments.
Experience with life science upstream source systems such as Veeva.




This position is offered in Spain and Denmark




Who is BASE life science?




Founded in 2007, BASE life science is a business and technology consulting enterprise working exclusively in the life sciences industry.

With headquarters in Denmark and offices across Europe and the United States, we support clients globally. We work closely with our customers to identify, develop, and implement initiatives that drive growth, efficiency, and meaningful change. Through partnerships with leading solution providers such as Veeva, Salesforce, IQVIA, Benchling, and MediSpend, we help our clients unlock the full potential of modern technologies.




Why join BASE Life Science?




At BASE, you work on meaningful, complex challenges at the intersection of technology, science, and business. We trust our people with real responsibility and support them in turning ambition into impact.




You will be part of a collaborative, high‑performing environment where:

Ambition is encouraged through ownership and continuous learning
Execution matters — we focus on delivering solutions that work in practice
Team spirit and trust guide collaboration across roles, countries, and disciplines
Openness means your ideas are heard, you are challenged constructively, and supported as you grow




Alongside this, we offer:

Support for health and wellbeing, covering physical, mental, and social needs
Flexible ways of working, built on trust, autonomy, and balance
Ongoing learning and professional development throughout your career
A modern work setup, with the tools and equipment needed to do great work
Recognition of performance and impact, linked to contribution and results
The opportunity to work on challenges that make a meaningful difference for patients and healthcare systems worldwide




Explore our work, values, and people at www.baselifescience.com







Interested?

If you find this position intriguing, don't delay—submit your application in English at your earliest convenience. We are continuously reviewing and assessing all incoming candidates and eagerly await your application.

At BASE Life Science, we value diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. Employment decisions are based solely on qualifications, merit, and business needs




By submitting your application, you consent to BASE Life Science processing your personal data for recruitment and selection purposes, in accordance with our Recruitment Candidate Privacy Notice and Privacy Policy.

Title: AI Engineer Consultant
Description:
Introduction

Vil du hjælpe nogle af Danmarks største virksomheder med at realisere deres AI-potentiale? Vi søger en nysgerrig og ambitiøs AI Engineer til vores konsulentteam, som brænder for at omsætte data og AI til konkrete forretningsresultater. Rollen spænder bredt - fra klassisk data science og machine learning til de nyeste muligheder inden for Generativ AI og AI-agenter.

IBM Client Innovation Center er en del af IBM Consulting og leverer rådgivning, udvikling og implementering af innovative teknologiløsninger til både danske og internationale virksomheder. Vi kombinerer dyb teknisk ekspertise med stærk forretningsforståelse og hjælper vores kunder med at accelerere digital transformation, innovation og værdiskabelse.

Hos os får du mulighed for at arbejde med de nyeste AI-teknologier og være med til at udvikle løsninger, der skaber reel værdi for nogle af Danmarks mest spændende organisationer.

Your Role And Responsibilities

Som AI Engineer hos os bliver du en del af et stærkt fagligt miljø, hvor vi hjælper kunderne med at løfte deres tekniske fundament og skabe målbar værdi gennem AI. Du vil arbejde tæt sammen med kunderne om at designe, udvikle og implementere løsninger, der spænder fra dataplatforme og Databricks-pipelines til machine learning-modeller, AI-løsninger og LLM-baserede AI-agenter.

Hos os får du det bedste fra begge verdener. Mange af vores projekter løber over flere år og giver mulighed for at skabe langsigtet værdi for kunden, mens andre er kortere og mere intensive. Det betyder, at du løbende bliver eksponeret for nye brancher, teknologier og forretningsudfordringer - og udvikler dig hurtigt både fagligt og personligt.

Dine Opgaver Vil Blandt Andet Være At


Arbejde med de nyeste teknologier inden for AI, Generativ AI og Agentic AI.
Designe, udvikle og operationalisere machine learning- og AI-løsninger i produktionsmiljøer.
Udvikle intelligente applikationer ved hjælp af LLM’er, RAG, AI-agenter og Copilot-teknologier.
Bygge robuste data- og AI-pipelines på moderne cloud-platforme som Azure og Databricks.
Samarbejde tæt med kunder om at omsætte forretningsmæssige udfordringer til skalerbare tekniske løsninger.
Bidrage til arkitektur, teknologivalg og best practices inden for moderne AI-udvikling.
Holde dig opdateret på nye teknologier og aktivt bidrage til at styrke vores AI-kompetencer og innovationsarbejde.


Required Technical And Professional Expertise

For at få succes i rollen forestiller vi os at du:


Har erfaring inden for data engineering, data science eller AI – typisk 3-4 års erfaring eller er en stærk profil med potentiale til hurtigt at udvikle sig mod seniorniveau.
Har solid forståelse for machine learning, deep learning, AI (herunder Generativ og Agentic AI) samt stærke kompetencer i Python og SQL.
Har kendskab til Databricks og moderne data- og AI-arkitekturer.
Har praktisk erfaring med Azure Data & AI-platformen og trives med at bygge robuste løsninger i pro-code miljøer fremfor low-code/no-code.
Er nysgerrig, læringsorienteret og motiveres af tæt samarbejde med både kunder og kolleger.
Kommunikerer klart og professionelt på både dansk og engelsk.


Vi tilbyder

Hos IBM får du mulighed for at arbejde i frontlinjen af teknologi og innovation. Du bliver en del af et fællesskab, hvor læring, videndeling og personlig udvikling er en naturlig del af hverdagen. Samtidig får du adgang til et stærkt netværk og mulighed for at lære af nogle af branchens mest erfarne specialister og ledere.

Desuden Tilbyder Vi


Et stærkt fagligt miljø, hvor ekspertise går hånd i hånd med et inkluderende og socialt fællesskab.
Adgang til et af branchens stærkeste læringsmiljøer med træning, certificeringer og kontinuerlig kompetenceudvikling gennem IBMs globale læringsplatform for mere end 250.000 IBM-medarbejdere, så du hele tiden kan udvikle dig i takt med nye teknologier, markeder og kundebehov.
Spændende projekter, der giver variation, ansvar og mulighed for at skabe reel værdi fra dag ét.
Dygtige kolleger og en ledelse, der prioriterer både faglig udvikling, trivsel og arbejdsglæde.
Fleksibilitet og frihed under ansvar i et dynamisk og tillidsbaseret arbejdsmiljø.
En arbejdsplads med base på Aarhus Ø samt mulighed for at arbejde tæt sammen med kunder på tværs af brancher og lokationer.


Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, så er du velkommen til at kontakte Data & AI Practice Lead, Anders Schaumann på anders.schauman@ibm.com.
