Title: Advanced Analytics Specialist, Supply Chain Intelligence
Place: Ballerup, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you passionate about driving high-impact projects in a global supply chain? Do you want to play a key role in delivering transformational initiatives that shape how Ambu scales, operates, and delivers value to patients worldwide?

Ambu is transforming – and this is your opportunity to shape how intelligence drives our supply chain.

We have recently evolved our analytics function into a Supply Chain Intelligence team within our Global Supply Chain organization, and we are seeking an Advanced Analytics Specialist to join us. The team’s ambition is clear: moving from describing what happened to knowing what to do next. You will play a central role in delivering this journey; building predictive and prescriptive models, enabling what-if scenarios and decision support, and strengthening the data foundation that underpins it all; while helping build a data-driven culture across the global supply chain.

At Ambu, we are on an ambitious growth journey - scaling our operations and capabilities to meet the rising global demand for our innovative medical solutions. As part of this journey, you will join a highly motivated team that values initiative, impact, and collaboration; and you will have a front-row seat in maturing how data and intelligence power our global supply chain.

The role

As an Advanced Analytics Specialist, your primary responsibility is to design, build, and deploy analytics solutions that move our global supply chain from descriptive dashboards to predictive and prescriptive intelligence. You will work across our five focus areas: data foundation, reporting & visibility, advanced analytics, decision support, and analytics enablement; with the greatest emphasis on advanced analytics and decision support.

This is a hands-on role where technical depth meets business-centric problem-solving. Working closely with the Advanced Analytics Manager, GSC, you will partner with stakeholders across corporate functions and manufacturing sites – bridging business and IT – to ensure that data becomes a strategic asset embedded in how Ambu’s supply chain makes decisions.

Key Responsibilities

Advanced analytics & decision support (primary focus)


Develop, deploy, and maintain predictive and prescriptive models (e.g., forecasting, anomaly detection, optimization) that address concrete supply chain challenges across planning, operations, and distribution
Build what-if scenario and simulation capabilities that give leadership actionable decision support; not just reporting
Translate ambiguous business questions into structured analytical problems, and analytical results into clear recommendations for stakeholders and leadership
Automate recurring analyses and workflows to free capacity across the organization for higher-value work
Take ownership of solutions end-to-end: from problem framing and data preparation to model deployment in collaboration with back-end IT, monitoring, and adoption


Data foundation, visibility & analytics enablement


Contribute to advancing our data foundation toward a single source of truth; improving data quality, governance, and data models in collaboration with IT
Develop and enhance Power BI dashboards and standard GSC reporting where they create genuine visibility and value
Support upskilling across the global supply chain through training, documentation, and self-service enablement; helping embed a data-driven culture
Stay curious about new technologies (AI, machine learning, modern analytics tooling) and proactively bring relevant opportunities into the team


Suggested candidate profile

Skills

Skills and experience can be gained in many ways, and we understand that the best fit for the role cannot be described on paper. You are, however, likely to succeed in this position if you have:


2–5 years of experience in analytics, data science, or business intelligence roles. You may have gained your experience in a consultancy, an industrial company, or a data-driven scale-up
Strong hands-on skills in SQL and Python (or R) for data preparation, analysis, and modelling
Solid experience with Power BI – including DAX and data modelling – and an eye for visualizations that drive decisions
Experience building predictive models (e.g., forecasting or machine learning) and bringing at least one into real operational use; you know the difference between a notebook and a solution people rely on
Strong business translation skills; you can turn a vague business question into an analytical approach, and an analytical result into a clear recommendation
A working understanding of supply chain processes (e.g., planning, manufacturing, logistics, or distribution); you don't need to be a supply chain expert, but you can speak the language of operations and understand the physical flows behind the data
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills; you build trust quickly and collaborate effectively across functions and levels
A proactive, structured and independent way of working: you thrive in a maturing environment where you help build the way, not just follow it


It is an advantage if you have experience with the following:


Deeper experience within supply chain planning, S&OP, or distribution analytics
ERP data (e.g., Infor), cloud data platforms (e.g., Azure, Fabric), or optimization and simulation techniques
Experience in integrating new data (e.g., through API/EDI) into database and semantic models
Experience in training or upskilling non-technical colleagues
Exposure to a GxP-regulated industry


Please note that due to the upcoming summer holiday period, the recruitment process may take slightly longer than usual. We expect to begin the interview process at the beginning of August.

Ambu – a visionary and international workplace where your efforts matter

Ambu is a company that expands rapidly and has ambitious growth targets. We meet these targets through interdisciplinary teamwork between motivated and highly skilled employees. Your contributions and efforts are crucial to our success and by joining us you will get the opportunity to work in an international head office with an informal working environment.

We offer great opportunities for personal development. Furthermore, we offer a wide range of professional, social and financial employee benefits in addition to exciting job challenges and continuous professional and personal development.

Your contributions and efforts are crucial to our success and by joining us you will get the opportunity to work in an international head office with an informal working environment.

We offer great opportunities for personal development. Furthermore, we offer a wide range of professional, social and financial employee benefits in addition to exciting job challenges and continuous professional and personal development.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Spirii develops world-class charging products and digital solutions for electric vehicles. Our Digital Development department fosters a listen, learn, and lead culture — allowing us to continuously improve how we work. We're looking for a hands-on Data Engineer to build and evolve Spirii's data platform and pipeline infrastructure. Working closely with our Data Lead and domain teams, you'll design and maintain reliable ELT pipelines, ensure data arrives in Amazon Redshift correctly and on time, and support the analytics layer that powers insights across EV charging events, subscriptions, and financial transactions. This is a unique opportunity to shape Spirii's data infrastructure from an early stage — establishing standards for ingestion, orchestration, and data reliability as we scale.

Your Responsibilities

Pipeline & Ingestion


Design, build, and maintain scalable ELT pipelines that bring data from product backends, third-party services, and operational systems into Amazon Redshift.
Own ingestion reliability: monitoring, alerting, and incident response for pipeline failures.
Evaluate and improve orchestration tooling and scheduling.


Data Platform & Infrastructure


Evolve Spirii's data platform architecture: storage, compute, access control, and cost optimisation in Redshift.
Implement and maintain infrastructure-as-code practices for data workflows (Terraform/CDK or similar).
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for data infrastructure and transformations.


Data Quality & Governance


Implement data quality checks, freshness monitoring, and anomaly detection across ingestion and transformation layers.
Work with Analytics Engineers and domain teams to define and enforce data contracts and schema standards.
Ensure GDPR-compliant data handling: PII identification, access control, and data retention policies.


Collaboration & Stakeholders


Partner with Engineering and Product teams to instrument events correctly at the source — schemas, identifiers, and change management.
Collaborate with Data team to ensure the analytics layer is built on reliable, well-modelled foundations.
Contribute to a strong data culture by creating and enhancing documentation, runbooks, data lineage, discoverability and incident response capabilities.


About You


You care deeply about data reliability and building pipelines that teams can depend on.
You're hands-on: you build, test, and ship pipelines and infrastructure improvements yourself.
You think of data pipelines as products — you care about uptime, observability, and the downstream consumers who depend on them.
You're pragmatic: you know when to engineer robustly and when to keep things lean.
You collaborate well across teams and communicate clearly about trade-offs and status.


Qualifications


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
3+ years of experience in data engineering or a related role with a strong data focus.
Strong SQL skills and solid experience building production data pipelines.
Hands-on experience with a modern data warehouse (Redshift preferred; BigQuery/Snowflake/Databricks also relevant).
Experience with orchestration tools (Apache Airflow or similar).
Proficiency in Python for data work (pipeline scripts, transformations, tooling).
Solid understanding of ELT/ETL concepts, including incremental loading, CDC, and idempotency.
Familiar with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, CDK, or similar).
Familiar with data governance concepts: access control, PII handling, GDPR-minded practices.


Nice to Have


Experience with data observability tooling.
Experience with event/data contract patterns in product analytics (schemas, versioning, instrumentation).
Experience with finance data and/or subscription/transaction domains.
Experience with dbt (understanding of transformation layer and how it interfaces with pipelines).
Machine learning infrastructure experience is a plus, but not required for this role.


Benefits

It is important for us to create a healthy, diverse, fun, and creative working environment, so besides all the basic benefits, we also focus a lot on social activities, flexible working hours and a good work-life balance.


Flexible Work Environment: Enjoy a collaborative, inclusive, and open workplace designed to fit your needs and support work-life balance.
Competitive Package: A competitive salary, an 11% company pension plan, and a comprehensive benefits package.
Time to Recharge: Benefit from 32 paid vacation days (5 weeks + 5 extra) and paid parental leave to focus on what matters most.
Career Growth: Access professional development opportunities and grow your career in a high-impact, high-growth industry.
Stay Energized: Enjoy excellent coffee, tea, free snacks, and subsidized lunches at the office.
Stay Active: Use our Spirii gym and shared company car to keep moving.
Make an Impact: Be part of a team driving the future of sustainable energy while working on meaningful projects.
Two innovation days per month for YOU to choose what you work on.


The position is a permanent position in Copenhagen, Denmark. Danish language is not a requirement, but fluent English language skills are required.

About Spirii – Who We Are

Spirii is a full-service e-Mobility provider, which offers hardware and software charging solutions to companies in the private and the public sector. Spirii has recently celebrated its six-year anniversary as a founded company; however the people in Spirii have extensive experience working with electric cars and charging, as well as the development of future transport and energy systems.

Spirii is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences. Spirii strives to create an inclusive and diverse workplace, and we encourage all qualified applicants to apply regardless of age, gender, race, sexual/religious orientation, or disability.

Title: AI Data Engineer, Forsvarets AI Center
Description:
Bygger du datapipelines og data-infrastruktur — og vil du være med til at gøre Forsvarets operative data anvendeligt for AI på tværs af klassifikationsniveauer? Så læs videre.




Om Forsvarets AI-Center

AI er ved at omdefinere både kamppladsen og den måde, Forsvaret skaber og anvender kampkraft. AI forkorter vejen fra observation til beslutning og virkning og åbner for opgaveløsninger, der ikke tidligere var mulige. AI er ikke noget, der blot understøtter Forsvarets operationer — AI er med til at definere dem. Det er derfor, AI har en central plads i Forsvaret, og det er derfor, Forsvarets AI-Center er etableret som ny enhed i Forsvarskommandoen.




Forsvarets AI-Center sætter retning for, hvordan Forsvaret anvender AI, så investeringer omsættes til reelt dansk og allieret forsvarsevne. Vi prioriterer operative behov på tværs af værn og kommandoer i tæt dialog med operatørerne, og vi opbygger partnerskaber med NATO, allierede, akademia og industri. Vores opgave er at sikre, at AI bliver til kampkraft — ikke teknologi for teknologiens skyld.




Vi arbejder AI-først og innovativt nok til at ændre os, mens virkeligheden ændrer sig. Det betyder korte cyklusser, tæt på operatøren, og tempo og læring som operative krav. Vi udvikler i åbne miljøer for at få fart og indsigt, og vi tager løsninger i drift i klassificerede miljøer, hvor sikkerhed og operativ relevans er afgørende. Vi har adgang til noget af Nordens bedste compute, og vores infrastruktur er bygget til, at AI er en del af løsningen fra start.




Forsvarets AI-Center er stedet, hvor Forsvarets AI-fremtid formes.




Om stillingen

Som AI Data Engineer i Forsvarets AI Center bygger du fundamentet under det som centeret leverer - datapipelines og infrastruktur der gør operative datakilder anvendelige for AI på tværs af klassifikationsniveauer. Du arbejder tæt sammen med AI Software Engineers, AI Integrations Engineers og operative enheder, og vurderer hvordan nye datakilder kan omsættes til reel operativ værdi. Rollen er for dig der trives i mødet mellem dataengineering, sikkerhedskrav og operative realiteter - og som motiveres af at det data du arbejder med ender med at gøre en forskel i felten.




Af vigtigste opgaver kan nævnes

Designe, etablere og vedligeholde datapipelines og datainfrastruktur, der indsamler, transformerer, lagrer og leverer data fra Forsvarets operative kilder til AI-modeller og applikationer.
Implementere og videreudvikle data lakes, data warehouses og øvrige dataplatforme, der understøtter sikker og effektiv adgang til data på tværs af klassifikationsniveauer.
Vurdere datakilders kvalitet, anvendelighed og tilgængelighed i relation til AI-use cases og operative behov.
Samarbejde med AI Software Engineers, AI Integration Engineers, Platform Engineers og øvrige interessenter om datakvalitet, datatilgængelighed og infrastrukturelle behov.
Koordinere med Forsvarets relevante myndigheder og operative enheder om adgang til og anvendelse af operative datakilder.




Vi tilbyder dig at bygge data-infrastrukturen i et AI Center der er nyt nok til at lytte til dig og bredt nok til at dine pipelines kommer i drift hos operatører på tværs af Forsvarets værn. Du arbejder med klassificerede miljøer og foundation models i en kombination der findes meget få steder.




Om dig

Du har en relevant uddannelse inden for datalogi, data science, software engineering eller et tilsvarende område. Alternativt har du dokumenteret erfaring og stærke resultater inden for data engineering. Du har erfaring med design, udvikling og drift af moderne dataplatforme, herunder datapipeline-orchestration, data lakes, data warehouses samt ETL- og ELT-løsninger.




Derudover besidder du stærke kompetencer inden for SQL og behersker mindst ét moderne programmeringssprog til data engineering, såsom Python, Scala eller Go. Du har erfaring med datakvalitet, validering, governance og metadatahåndtering i komplekse datamiljøer og forstår, hvordan operative data omsættes til AI-trænings- og produktionsdata. Samtidig er du i stand til at samarbejde tæt med AI-udviklere om deres databehov. Du behersker både dansk og engelsk på professionelt niveau. Det er en fordel, hvis du har erfaring fra eller kendskab til Forsvarets operative virkelighed. Erfaring med Sovereign Cloud, cloud-native platforme eller klassificerede datamiljøer vil ligeledes være en styrke.




Ansættelsesvilkår

Du ansættes i henhold til den til enhver tid gældende overenskomst. Ved lønfastsættelsen vil der blive lagt vægt på erfaring, kompetencer og stillingens indhold. Der er til stillingen knyttet en arbejdsgiverfinansieret pensionsordning.




Arbejdsstedet er i Storkøbenhavn.




Som ansat under Forsvarsministeriets område er du omfattet af nogle særlige vilkår. Du skal bl.a. kunne sikkerhedsgodkendes og opretholde sikkerhedsgodkendelsen under hele ansættelsesforholdet.




Du har som ansat pligt til at forrette tjeneste uden for landets grænser, herunder ved enheder i internationale operationer med henblik på deltagelse i konfliktforebyggende, fredsbevarende, fredsskabende, humanitære og andre lignende operationer.




Kontakt og ansøgning

Hvis du vil høre mere om stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Næstkommanderende for Forsvarets AI Center, Major Rasmus Kilmose på telefon 2960 0486.




Har du spørgsmål til løn- og ansættelsesvilkår er du velkommen til at kontakte Personelkommandoen, HR-konsulent Madeleine Moltke Kæmsgaard på telefon 4031 1094.




Ansøgningsfristen er den 13. juli 2026. Samtaler og ansættelse sker løbende.




Du søger stillingen via linket og vi beder dig vedhæfte ansøgning, CV og kopi af uddannelsesbevis. Vær opmærksom på, at bilag skal tilføjes i PDF-format.




I Forsvarsministeriets organisation tror vi på, at mangfoldighed optimerer opgaveløsningen. Derfor opfordrer vi alle interesserede uanset baggrund til at søge stillingen.




Om Forsvarets AI-Center (FAIC)

AI er ved at omdefinere både kamppladsen og den måde, Forsvaret skaber og anvender kampkraft. AI forkorter vejen fra observation til beslutning og virkning og åbner for opgaveløsninger, der ikke tidligere var mulige. AI er ikke noget, der blot understøtter Forsvarets operationer — AI er med til at definere dem. Det er derfor, AI har en central plads i Forsvaret, og det er derfor, Forsvarets AI-Center (FAIC) er etableret som ny enhed i Forsvarskommandoen.




FAIC sætter retning for, hvordan Forsvaret anvender AI, så investeringer omsættes til reel dansk og allieret forsvarsevne. Vi prioriterer operative behov på tværs af værn og kommandoer i tæt dialog med operatørerne, og vi opbygger partnerskaber med NATO, allierede, akademia og industri. Vores opgave er at sikre, at AI bliver til kampkraft — ikke teknologi for teknologiens skyld.




Vi arbejder AI-først og innovativt nok til at ændre os, mens virkeligheden ændrer sig. Det betyder korte cyklusser, tæt på operatøren, og tempo og læring som operative krav. Vi udvikler i åbne miljøer for at få fart og indsigt, og vi tager løsninger i drift i klassificerede miljøer, hvor sikkerhed og operativ relevans er afgørende. Vi har adgang til noget af Nordens bedste compute, og vores infrastruktur er bygget til, at AI er en del af løsningen fra start.

Title: Quantitative analyst for Short-term Power Trading
Description:
Experienced in quantitative modeling for energy or finance? Skilled in programming? Join us as a quantitative analyst for Intraday Analysis and make a real impact.




Here’s what the job entails

In this role, you will help optimise our trading performance by developing quantitative models that impact the decision-making process across our trading teams.




More specifically, your responsibilities include:

developing and enhancing quantitative models to drive trading decisions
taking active part in coding algorithmic trading infrastructure and optimisation engines used for live trading
conducting market research and strategy analysis aimed at improving overall PnL and operational robustness
collaborating with team members to ensure the operational stability of algorithms and tools developed by the team




Meet the team

As our new quantitative analyst, you will join our Intraday Analysis team, where we support the trading and asset optimisation teams by building quantitative models and trading tools. Our primary area of responsibility is to develop strategies, trading tools, and quantitative models that enable decision-making for our proprietary trading and asset business.




We are a team of 6 specialists in energy trading with backgrounds in economics and mathematics. We are a curious, dedicated, and highly analytical team who always strive to improve processes, systems, and strategies to fine-tune the infrastructural engine of our power trading business.




But enough about us… over to you!

As you will be developing models and analyses used for decision-making, we are looking for someone who has a data-driven approach and an eye for market risks while also evaluating other factors in your analyses. Also, as we operate in an ever-changing market, we see you as someone who thrives in a dynamic environment and can quickly adapt to market conditions.




We also imagine that you:

have an educational background in a quantitative field, for example, economics, mathematics, physics, statistics, data science, or engineering
have experience in a similar position, either in the energy industry, the financial markets or similar
have solid coding skills with the ability to write production-level Python code

Title: Data Science & BI Developer - 12 month maternity cover
Place: Hørsholm, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Join our Golf Insights team and work hands-on with one of the world’s richest data ecosystems in golf. You’ll be appointed to specific product segments and deliver insights that foster data-driven decisions internally and external customers.

Our team is responsible for the transformational data modelling utilized in different consumption layers, driving meaningful accessible insights. We are on a continuous journey to strengthen our foundation of augmented analytics, which you will be expected to contribute to.

We collaborate closely with the product teams as well as various R&D functions, to deliver insights that drive product development and customer business success.

Your Profile

We Are Looking For a Data Science & BI Developer To Join Our Team On a 12-month Maternity Cover Contract. We Expect You To Have 3+ Years Of Experience In BI And Analytics Or Related Fields. Golf Knowledge And Passion For The Sport Is a Plus, While We Also Expect That You Are


Motivated, proactive, and a fast learner 
Curious about working with complex data 
Enthusiastic about using AI tools to work smarter and build better
Excited by the idea of building high-quality BI tools that have a real-world impact 
Eager to grow in an international, collaborative, and sports-driven environment 


You have some hands-on experience with SQL and dashboards (Power BI or similar), and ideally some exposure to Microsoft Fabric. You’re comfortable working with data from initial transformations to final reports, and you're energized by the opportunity to bring AI into how insights are created and consumed. 

Responsibilities


Build and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports 
Leverage AI development tools to accelerate BI deliverables and improve output quality
Explore and implement augmented analytics capabilities to enhance how end users interact with data
Develop and optimize data models and schemas 
Collaborate with data engineers to acquire and prepare data 
Work with product owners and internal stakeholders to define BI needs 


Job Requirements


A master's degree in Business Intelligence, Computer Science, Data Analytics or a similar data-related field
Experience writing SQL and working with relational databases 
Proficiency with Power BI or other dashboarding tools, while broader experience in Microsoft Fabric is a plus
Experience with dbt
A proactive, independent approach – you enjoy owning your tasks and learning as you go 
Fluent in English and comfortable working in a cross-cultural team 


The Office

We are located at DTU Science Park in Hørsholm. We take great pride in the state-of-art building we have designed! The office features bright meeting rooms, coffee areas to catch up with colleagues, a large testing arena with simulators available for employees and a fitness center, everything surrounded by a beautiful forest and respecting the environment. We made sure to utilize the newest technologies to be energy efficient and be mindful of ventilation and sound propagation. The cherry on top is our canteen: with an indoor and outdoor area to enjoy the fresh food prepared by our own kitchen staff.

Join the home of a powerful sports brand and a one-of-a-kind technology

Our proprietary technology is based on expert knowledge about radar, computer vision, data, and software engineering. Our solutions are developed by specialists who endlessly explore and challenge new technical boundaries. Trackman’s blend of cutting-edge technology, sports, and continued growth make our company an outstanding place to work.

Our work culture is entrepreneurial, ambitious, and rewarding as you get to collaborate with inspiring colleagues and interact with the leaders of golf, baseball, and football. Based on a unique brand reputation, we are proud to work in close partnership with the top athletes, organizations, and teams that rely on our technology to stay ahead of the race.

We are a Danish company founded, owned, and managed since 2003 by three entrepreneurs with a strong passion for sports. Today, we have a global footprint with our headquarters located in Denmark (Hørsholm) and offices in New York, Phoenix, Seoul and Tokyo. We employ over 1000+ people worldwide, half of which work in Denmark, where most hardware and software products are developed, tested, and manufactured.

Where innovation happens

At Trackman, we know that great people make great products. We believe that teams thrive, and innovation sparks in an atmosphere where people are encouraged to think for themselves, where everybody’s voices are heard, and where the best ideas prevail in the pursuit of exceptional solutions. This mix makes Trackman an inspiring company – for our customers and colleagues.

Share our Passion!

Applications are continuously assessed, so please send your application as soon as possible. For additional information about the position, please contact hiring manager; Mikkel Hansen at mha@trackman.com .

Title: Product Analytics Engineer
Place: Aarhus Municipality, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
The role

We are looking for a Product Analytics Engineer to join our product team in Aarhus.

This is not a role where you only build pipelines. You own Candeno's data and analytics layer within your domain: the data warehouse, the BI and dashboards, and the instrumentation that turns product usage and AI interactions into reliable data. Part of the work is figuring out the right question before you build the answer. The rest is building it well.




We work AI-augmented. Modern AI tools are a natural part of how we work, so more of the routine building is handled for you. That makes the harder, more valuable work the judgement: what to measure, how to model it, and whether the numbers can be trusted. We use AI to move faster, and we believe people are what make sure we build the right things and trust the right data.




Your work will reach hundreds of thousands of people across the Nordics: job seekers navigating labour market reforms, employment professionals adapting to new digitalisation strategies, and the institutions managing that change. The dashboards and data you build inform decisions inside Candeno and in the hands of our customers, so keeping them trustworthy matters.




What you will work on

Candeno serves municipalities, unemployment funds, and educational institutions across Denmark, Norway, Sweden, and Finland. The platform has three sides: candidates navigating the labour market, advisors guiding them through digitalisation and compliance workflows, and companies recruiting talent. One platform, many contexts. Our products increasingly run on data and AI, and this role builds the foundation underneath them.




Discovery (analysis and design)

•    Work with product people and customers to understand what a dashboard or a data model actually needs to answer, before you build it. AI helps us move fast, so the harder work is knowing what is worth measuring.

•    Model the data. Decide access patterns and weigh the tradeoffs between flexibility and control, with architecture direction from our Lead Architect.




Delivery (build and operate)

•    Build and maintain the data warehouse, data pipelines, and ETL or ELT flows between Candeno's platform and the warehouse.

•    Build and maintain internal and customer-facing dashboards, so the right data reaches the right audience in a form that drives decisions.

•    Instrument our AI chats, agents, and product usage so requests and usage signals become structured, first-class data, and work with engineers to keep it accessible for AI and ML use.

•    Write clean, maintainable code, using AI tools where they help and applying judgement to what they produce.




Quality and craft

•    Keep data trustworthy. Care about data quality and make it something the team can rely on.

•    Meet regulatory requirements in a data-sensitive sector, with guidance from our legal advisor.

•    Share what you know and help raise data competence across the team. This is knowledge-sharing, not people management.




Who we are looking for

You are a data and analytics engineer who is as comfortable with the question as with the query. Your foundation is solid delivery in data and BI, and your edge is knowing what is worth measuring before you build it.




You will need:

•    A few years building and maintaining data warehouses and BI or dashboards, ideally in SaaS or a product company. Full build experience matters more than years.

•    Solid data modelling skills, and comfort navigating the tradeoffs between flexibility and control.

•    Experience building data pipelines and ETL or ELT flows. Modern transformation tooling such as dbt is a plus.

•    An understanding of what AI and ML need from data, in structure and quality. You can grow into this.

•    A considered approach to AI in your workflow. You use modern tools well, and you check what they produce critically.

•    Clear communication. You can explain a data tradeoff to a product manager, an engineer, and a customer, and make it feel simple.

•    Comfort working with customers directly.




The kind of person who does well here:

•    Curious about the question, not only the query.

•    Ownership mindset. You follow a problem all the way through.

•    Comfortable making decisions without a perfect foundation, and honest about the tradeoffs when you do.

•    Direct. You share work early, give feedback clearly, and push back when something is not right.




What the first six months look like

After one month

You understand Candeno's platform, our BI setup and data flows, and the team. You have taken ownership of your first scoped tasks and you are active in how the team works.




After three months

You are building key parts of the warehouse and BI independently and contributing to product and AI instrumentation. You are starting to engage with customer analytics needs, with support.




After six months

You reliably own and deliver within the data and analytics domain. The dashboards and instrumentation you build are in real use, and you are a dependable go-to for product analytics questions, working within architecture direction from the Lead Architect.




Working at Candeno

We are a scale-up. That means real ownership, real impact, and a product that is still being shaped by the people who work on it.

We are based in Aarhus, and this is an on-site role. The team is small, cross-functional, and works closely together. You will have real ownership of your work and close proximity to product, engineering, and our customers.

We are direct and focused on doing the right thing for users. We share work early and expect you to do the same.




What we offer

•    Pension scheme and health insurance

•    5 extra days off after 2 years of seniority (10 in total)

•    Daily lunch from Meyers Madhus

•    A shared milestone programme that celebrates successes big and small

•    An active social life: team events, clubs, and an engaged social committee




The process

•    A short screening call

•    A first interview, a deeper conversation about your experience and the role

•    A case interview, a practical brief sent in advance

No cover letter. You will answer three short questions when you apply.




About Candeno

Candeno is a Nordic employment technology company based in Aarhus. We build the platform that connects candidates, advisors, and companies across municipalities, unemployment funds, and educational institutions in Denmark, Norway, Sweden, and Finland. Our work sits at the intersection of public sector compliance and modern product thinking, and we take both seriously.

Title: AI Udvikler
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Bliv AI-udvikler hos KOMBIT og vær med til at udvikle fremtidens AI-løsninger til kommunerne!




Har du lyst til at udvikle ansvarlige, sikre og værdiskabende AI-løsninger – og arbejde med de nyeste værktøjer i et stærkt fagligt miljø? Så har vi netop den rette mulighed for dig.




Et stærkt it-fællesskab

I KOMBIT har de danske kommuner et fælles omdrejningspunkt med stærke og specialiserede kompetencer på digitaliseringsområdet. Vi er et kommunalt ejet it-fællesskab, der er ansvarlig for at indkøbe, forvalte, drifte og videreudvikle tidssvarende og stabile it-løsninger og -infrastruktur til gavn for borgere og virksomheder. Sammen med kommunerne og it-leverandørerne arbejder vi for, at borgere og virksomheder i Danmark får en bedre service og billigere og mere sammenhængende it-løsninger. KOMBITs ambition er at sikre fortsat kommunal digital bevægelse - i dag og i fremtiden.




Vi har et stærkt fokus på at levere ansvarlige og sikre AI-løsninger, der kan komme i produktion og skabe reel værdi for kommunerne.




Dine primære ansvarsområder

Du bliver tilknyttet specialet Design & Arkitektur og en del af KOMBITs fokuserede AI core team. Her arbejder vi sammen med bl.a. KL, kommunerne, leverandører samt interne og eksterne eksperter om at udvikle, modne og udbrede AI-løsninger i den kommunale sektor.

Vi søger udviklere, der vil være med til at bygge konkrete AI-løsninger fra idé til produktion. Det kan være, at du har en baggrund inden for data science, erfaring som full stack-udvikler eller stærke kompetencer inden for DevOps, CI/CD og moderne udviklingsmiljøer. Det vigtigste er, at du har lyst til at arbejde praktisk og nysgerrigt med AI-teknologi, softwareudvikling og løsninger, der kan skabe reel værdi for kommunerne.




Som AI-udvikler hos KOMBIT kommer du bl.a. til at arbejde med udvikling, test, integration, drift og videreudvikling af AI-løsninger. Vi anvender AI aktivt i alle faser af udviklingen – fra analyse, design og kodning til test, dokumentation, kvalitetssikring og drift. Du vil derfor også få mulighed for at arbejde med AI-agenter og andre nye værktøjer, der kan styrke både udviklingsprocessen og de løsninger, vi leverer.




Du kan eksempelvis blive involveret i arbejdet med vores AI Assistent, som hjælper kommunale sagsbehandlere med at anvende fagsystemer og søge information i lovgivningen, og som også anvendes internt i KOMBIT. Derudover kan du bidrage til udvikling af nye AI-produkter, fælles komponenter og platforme, screening af nye use cases, dataanalyse, evaluering, overvågning og sikker drift af AI-løsninger.




Vi er på udkig efter flere nye kollegaer til teamet, så uanset om du primært ser dig selv som data scientist, full stack-udvikler, DevOps-profil eller AI-nysgerrig softwareudvikler, hører vi meget gerne fra dig.




Fokus på dig og dit (arbejds)liv

Vi tilbyder dig et job, hvor omdrejningspunktet er samarbejde på tværs, og hvor du vil arbejde med nogle af Danmarks største it-løsninger. Samtidig bliver du en del af en ambitiøs virksomhed, der står i udviklingens tegn, både hvad angår de mange eksisterende løsninger samt forventningen om en fremtid med endnu bredere portefølje inden for digitalisering og it-dreven innovation i kommunerne.




I KOMBIT er det vigtigt, at alle medarbejdere finder en god balance mellem arbejde og privatliv. Vi skaber motiverende rammer, der giver medarbejderne frihed til at planlægge og løse deres opgaver ud fra individuelle behov. Af samme grund vil du hurtigt opleve, at der er højt til loftet og rig mulighed for at sætte dit præg i en hver fyldt med spændende opgaver. Vi har som organisation fokus på dig og dit (arbejds)liv i balance – vi kalder det LiFE!

Lyder det som noget for dig?




Så tag første skridt mod en spændende karriere i frontlinjen for digital udvikling, og send os dit CV senest d. 2. august 2026. Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at sende en mail til Chef for Design & Arkitektur, Lars Vraa (LVR@kombit.dk).




Vi screener relevante kandidater, hvor vi spørger ind til motivationen for at søge stillingen samt forventninger til vilkår for at sikre, at vi finder den bedst egnede kandidat til stillingen.




Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig.

HTML:
Bliv AI-udvikler hos KOMBIT og vær med til at udvikle fremtidens AI-løsninger til kommunerne!


Har du lyst til at udvikle ansvarlige, sikre og værdiskabende AI-løsninger – og arbejde med de nyeste værktøjer i et stærkt fagligt miljø? Så har vi netop den rette mulighed for dig.


Et stærkt it-fællesskab

I KOMBIT har de danske kommuner et fælles omdrejningspunkt med stærke og specialiserede kompetencer på digitaliseringsområdet. Vi er et kommunalt ejet it-fællesskab, der er ansvarlig for at indkøbe, forvalte, drifte og videreudvikle tidssvarende og stabile it-løsninger og -infrastruktur til gavn for borgere og virksomheder. Sammen med kommunerne og it-leverandørerne arbejder vi for, at borgere og virksomheder i Danmark får en bedre service og billigere og mere sammenhængende it-løsninger. KOMBITs ambition er at sikre fortsat kommunal digital bevægelse - i dag og i fremtiden.


Vi har et stærkt fokus på at levere ansvarlige og sikre AI-løsninger, der kan komme i produktion og skabe reel værdi for kommunerne.


Dine primære ansvarsområder

Du bliver tilknyttet specialet Design & Arkitektur og en del af KOMBITs fokuserede AI core team. Her arbejder vi sammen med bl.a. KL, kommunerne, leverandører samt interne og eksterne eksperter om at udvikle, modne og udbrede AI-løsninger i den kommunale sektor.

Vi søger udviklere, der vil være med til at bygge konkrete AI-løsninger fra idé til produktion. Det kan være, at du har en baggrund inden for data science, erfaring som full stack-udvikler eller stærke kompetencer inden for DevOps, CI/CD og moderne udviklingsmiljøer. Det vigtigste er, at du har lyst til at arbejde praktisk og nysgerrigt med AI-teknologi, softwareudvikling og løsninger, der kan skabe reel værdi for kommunerne.


Som AI-udvikler hos KOMBIT kommer du bl.a. til at arbejde med udvikling, test, integration, drift og videreudvikling af AI-løsninger. Vi anvender AI aktivt i alle faser af udviklingen – fra analyse, design og kodning til test, dokumentation, kvalitetssikring og drift. Du vil derfor også få mulighed for at arbejde med AI-agenter og andre nye værktøjer, der kan styrke både udviklingsprocessen og de løsninger, vi leverer.


Du kan eksempelvis blive involveret i arbejdet med vores AI Assistent, som hjælper kommunale sagsbehandlere med at anvende fagsystemer og søge information i lovgivningen, og som også anvendes internt i KOMBIT. Derudover kan du bidrage til udvikling af nye AI-produkter, fælles komponenter og platforme, screening af nye use cases, dataanalyse, evaluering, overvågning og sikker drift af AI-løsninger.


Vi er på udkig efter flere nye kollegaer til teamet, så uanset om du primært ser dig selv som data scientist, full stack-udvikler, DevOps-profil eller AI-nysgerrig softwareudvikler, hører vi meget gerne fra dig.


Fokus på dig og dit (arbejds)liv

Vi tilbyder dig et job, hvor omdrejningspunktet er samarbejde på tværs, og hvor du vil arbejde med nogle af Danmarks største it-løsninger. Samtidig bliver du en del af en ambitiøs virksomhed, der står i udviklingens tegn, både hvad angår de mange eksisterende løsninger samt forventningen om en fremtid med endnu bredere portefølje inden for digitalisering og it-dreven innovation i kommunerne.


I KOMBIT er det vigtigt, at alle medarbejdere finder en god balance mellem arbejde og privatliv. Vi skaber motiverende rammer, der giver medarbejderne frihed til at planlægge og løse deres opgaver ud fra individuelle behov. Af samme grund vil du hurtigt opleve, at der er højt til loftet og rig mulighed for at sætte dit præg i en hver fyldt med spændende opgaver. Vi har som organisation fokus på dig og dit (arbejds)liv i balance – vi kalder det LiFE!

Lyder det som noget for dig?


Så tag første skridt mod en spændende karriere i frontlinjen for digital udvikling, og send os dit CV senest d. 2. august 2026. Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at sende en mail til Chef for Design & Arkitektur, Lars Vraa (LVR@kombit.dk).


Vi screener relevante kandidater, hvor vi spørger ind til motivationen for at søge stillingen samt forventninger til vilkår for at sikre, at vi finder den bedst egnede kandidat til stillingen.


Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig.

Title: Data Science - Manager - Retail
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

As a Manager for Data Science consulting, you operate at the intersection of business strategy, data, and emerging AI technologies. You work closely with clients to shape their data and AI agendas—translating business challenges into actionable opportunities, advising on the right approaches, and guiding initiatives from idea through to value realization.

You bring a strong technical foundation, but your primary focus is on understanding client needs, framing problems, and designing impactful solutions. You are a trusted advisor who can connect strategy, data, technology, and organizational change—while staying hands-on enough to explore data, prototype solutions, and leverage modern AI tools.

Qualifications


Strong experience in data, AI, or analytics roles, with exposure to both technical implementation and business-facing work
Proven ability to engage clients, structure problems, and translate business needs into data/AI solutions
Proven experience in developing and deploying AI models at scale, including natural language processing (NLP), computer vision, and reinforcement learning.
Expertise in machine learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, and scikit-learn, as well as experience with cloud-based AI platforms (e.g., AWS, GCP, Azure).
Strong understanding of data engineering concepts, including data pipelines and big data technologies such as Spark, Hadoop, SQL, and NoSQL.
Experience working with modern AI ecosystems (e.g., cloud platforms, LLMs, AI tools, or data platforms)
Strong communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders at multiple levels
Comfortable working in ambiguous environments, exploring new problem spaces, and expanding into adjacent domains
Demonstrated curiosity and eagerness to learn and apply new technologies, especially within the rapidly evolving AI landscape
Experience in consulting, digital transformation, or cross-functional environments is a strong plus


Additional Information

Set Yourself Apart With


Experience defining AI or data strategies for organizations
Hands-on experience with modern AI tools (e.g., generative AI, copilots, automation platforms)
Exposure to data architecture, data engineering, or product development
Track record of driving AI adoption or innovation initiatives within organizations
Advanced degree in a relevant field (e.g., data science, engineering, business, or similar)


What We’re Looking For

We’re looking for someone who is:


Equally comfortable in the boardroom and with a dataset
A translator between business ambition and technical reality
Curious, adaptable, and excited about the fast-moving AI landscape
Motivated to go beyond pure data science into broader problem-solving and innovation


Company Description

Publicis Sapient is a digital transformation partner helping established organizations get to their future, digitally enabled state, both in the way they work and the way they serve their customers. We help unlock value through a start-up mindset and modern methods, fusing strategy, consulting, and customer experience with agile engineering and problem-solving creativity. United by our core values and our purpose of helping people thrive in the brave pursuit of the next, our 20,000+ people in 53 offices around the world combine experience across technology, data sciences, consulting, and customer obsession to accelerate our clients’ businesses through designing the products and services their customers truly value.

Title: Quant Infrastructure Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Imagine working with cutting-edge machine learning models, designing robust and scalable software infrastructure, and collaborating with dedicated colleagues – all while playing a key role in scaling our business even further.




Does this sound exciting? Then you might be our new Quant Infrastructure Engineer at Alipes.




At Alipes, we have been a market leader in automating financial markets since our inception in 2008, pioneering fully automated natural language processing systems to read and interpret financial news. To accomplish our goal of being the best at what we do, we are focused on building a world-class ML engineering workflow, ensuring seamless training, deployment and monitoring of our predictive models.




As a core member of the Quant team, your work will be instrumental in developing the infrastructure enabling us to transform massive unstructured financial data sets into production-ready inference models that drive real-time trading decisions. You will work with technologies such as Python, Kubernetes, Ray and Airflow. In doing so, you will get a chance to develop and maintain the infrastructure needed for scalable, high-performance ML systems, ensuring a seamless pipeline from dataset generation through model prototyping to production deployment.




Key responsibilities include

Designing, optimizing and maturing our infrastructure to support distributed model training and experiment tracking, as well as further developing internal Python tools for smooth interaction.
Building and automating robust data pipelines for dataset generation and feature engineering, while enabling data version control.
Developing software for rapid experimentation and deployment, including automated testing, continuous integration, and delivery.
Improve feature generation system- develop a feature store with definition and automated generation.
Transitioning to a more structured table based dataset store.
Implementing best practices in software development, ensuring maintainability, scalability, and efficiency of ML systems through automated testing and real-time monitoring.
Improving model deployment workflows, focusing on minimizing latency, ensuring reproducibility, and enabling fast iterations.




You will get instant validation of your work and experience short feedback cycles, where going from inception to deployment can be a matter of hours. There will be no red tape to cut and no sales people to consult.




About the team

All of this will happen in an informal atmosphere where technical discussions are valued, and you are encouraged to take ownership of, and pride in, your work. You will impact the direction of the Quant team, prioritize your work and choose the tools that get the job done.




We are a tight-knit modeling team with passionate quantitative researchers covering 9 nationalities – a mix of PhD’s and MSc’s with expertise in statistical analysis, mathematical modeling and machine learning. We enjoy collaboration and are always willing to lend a helping hand. We are working alongside two other teams of software developers and traders that implement and conceive the mathematical models together with us. What makes this constellation work great is that all of the individual team members write code and are willing to engage on a deep, technical level of understanding.




About you

A scientific and inquisitive mind.
Fluency with computer science fundamentals, specifically data structures and algorithms.
Experience working with ‘out-of-core’ datasets.
Experience with Python, in particular the data pre-processing, ETL pipelines and distributed systems.
Familiarity with strongly typed languages like C# or C/C++ or Rust.
A few years of experience in production-grade environments working with Machine Learning based applications.
Experience with distributed computing frameworks (for example Spark, Ray or Dask).
Familiarity with containerization technologies (for example Docker Compose or Kubernetes).




Nice to have

Experience working with the full Machine Learning stack and a strong MLOps foundation, going from dataset generation through model training and real-life validation and monitoring.
Experience working with tools like PyTorch, Tensorflow, XGBoost and/or Catboost.
Familiarity with algorithmic trading concepts.
PhD or MSc degree in engineering, computer science, mathematics or physics.




What we can offer you

Competitive benefits, including pension, health insurance, and 30 days of vacation.
A modern office in Copenhagen (Nordhavn) with a flexible hybrid work model.
A flat hierarchy with high levels of trust, autonomy, and freedom to make decisions.
A valued role with unique responsibilities and plenty of room for creativity.
Fast feedback and recognition of your contributions in a high-growth company.
Strong team spirit - successes are celebrated with company events and trips.




Practicalities

To apply, please send us your CV. If your profile looks like a good match, we will get in touch to coordinate an online technical test as a first step in the interview process.




If you have specific questions about the role, you are welcome to email us at quant_jobs@alipescapital.com. Please do not send in your applications to this email address, as our interview team will not have visibility to review.

Title: Advanced Pricing Analyst
Place: Vejle, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join AutoProff – Help Build Trust in the Price Models Powering Our European Growth! 🚗

We’re looking for an Advanced Pricing Analyst to join our team, based out of our Vejle headquarters.

At AutoProff, pricing is at the heart of how we serve customers and drive commercial performance. As our pricing capabilities continue to evolve, we need stronger ownership of how our price models perform in production. This role will be central to creating transparency around pricing quality, spotting issues before they become escalations, and ensuring we understand why our models behave the way they do.

As Advanced Pricing Analyst, you will own the reporting, monitoring, and investigation of price model performance across our production setup. You will help define the right benchmarks and reporting views to evaluate performance over time, identify weakspots or unusual behaviour, and support both existing and new model evaluations. You will also take the lead on investigating specific pricing cases raised by Pricing teams, using data and system-level understanding to determine whether issues stem from data quality, model behaviour, system integration, or whether the model output is in fact well supported by market observations.

This role sits at the intersection of pricing, analytics, and technical investigation. You will work closely with Pricing, Data Science, and other stakeholders to ensure that pricing behaviour is monitored, understood, and communicated clearly across the organisation. You will also contribute to building and strengthening the pricing setup that supports AutoProff’s continued growth across Europe.

Let’s dive into your key responsibilities, you will


Own the reporting and monitoring framework for price model performance in production, including defining the KPIs, benchmarks, and views needed to create a clear overview of model quality and behaviour.
Analyse model performance across segments, markets, and versions to identify weakspots, anomalies, drift, and other signals of deteriorating quality before they become business issues.
Lead investigations into unusual price outcomes and determine whether the underlying cause relates to data quality, model behaviour, system integration, or stakeholder interpretation.
Build a strong understanding of how price models are productionised and consumed through APIs, and validate that models are being supplied with the correct data, format, and values.
Communicate findings and recommendations clearly to Pricing, Data Science, and management, while supporting the quality assurance and benchmarking of existing and new price models.


The need-to-have skills


Strong SQL skills and experience working independently with large datasets to build reporting, monitor performance, and investigate pricing behaviour.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and visualisation skills, with the ability to structure investigations and turn complex findings into clear and actionable insights.
Hands-on Python experience for data investigation, exploratory analysis, and technical debugging.
A good understanding of how machine learning models work in practice, including how input quality, feature behaviour, formatting issues, or drift can affect outputs.
Comfort working with APIs, payloads, and technical system flows, combined with strong communication skills and confidence in challenging assumptions with data and evidence.


The nice-to-have skills


Experience working with pricing models, recommendation models, or other predictive models in production.
Experience investigating model-serving issues, feature quality issues, or data pipeline issues affecting live model outputs.
Familiarity with model monitoring concepts such as drift detection, segmentation analysis, and performance benchmarking over time.
Ability to reproduce or inspect price requests locally through technical tools, services, or APIs.
Experience working closely with Data Science teams and translating between analytical, technical, and business perspectives.


We’re eager to hear from you! If this sounds like a great match and sparks your interest, don’t wait, apply today!

About AutoProff

AutoProff was founded in 2013 with the aim to digitalize B2B used car trading, helping car dealers increase sales and improve profits.

in August 2022, AutoScout24, the leading specialized pan-European online car marketplace, acquired a majority stake in AutoProff. The partnership has supported the rapid expansion of AutoProff's solutions across Europe and enables the creation of Europe's first fully integrated C2B-B2B-B2C dealing solution for used cars.

At AutoProff, we specialize in providing online B2B used-car dealing solutions designed to improve the buying and selling experience for professional car dealers and their customers.

We are the emerging European leader with more than 150,000 B2B used cars on our online trading platforms annually.

With a focus on effective solutions, digitization and customer service, we are committed to being the home of B2B car dealing for European car dealers.

Title: AI Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
As our next AI Engineer, you will contribute to solving meaningful challenges across the life sciences industry. We offer specialised management consulting services, working with top-tier life sciences companies as well as biotech startups — locally and globally. From strategy development to hands-on implementation, we support our clients were impact matters most.







About the role




Generative and agentic AI has moved from demos to production. With this, one of our key focus areas is building products with an agentic AI engine at their core that are reliable, secure, auditable, and good enough to put in front of a medical writer or a regulatory reviewer.




That challenge has two halves:




The AI engine: Orchestration, retrieval, context engineering, tool use, guardrails, and the evals and observability that make an agentic system able to solve business-critical tasks in a regulated environment.
The product around it: The UI, APIs, services, data layers, integrations, and deployment that turn that engine into robust, secure, scalable software customers depend on.




Today these two halves often pull against each other. We are building a team where they pull together. As an AI Engineer, you will focus on the AI engine itself: designing, building, evaluating, and improving agentic systems that solve real business problems in highly regulated environments.

Your responsibilities include:




Design, build, and ship AI-powered tools that help life science teams create, review, and manage critical documents and data.
Build features such as grounded retrieval, summarisation, document comparison, question answering, and content classification with traceability back to source.
Design agentic workflows capable of handling complex business processes.
Build evaluation frameworks and observability capabilities that ensure reliability and quality.
Ensure solutions are secure, auditable, and scalable in regulated cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GCP).
Collaborate closely with clinical, regulatory, quality, and commercial experts to understand their workflows and implement AI solutions that create measurable value.




You bring:




Strong Python skills and solid software engineering fundamentals.
Hands-on experience with at least one major cloud (AWS, Azure, or GCP).
Deep, hands-on experience with enterprise LLMs and their APIs (e.g. Anthropic/Claude, OpenAI, Google), including tool/function calling, structured outputs, and the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
Experience building agentic systems with modern orchestration frameworks such as Pydantic AI, LangGraph, OpenAI Agents SDK, AutoGen / Microsoft Agent Framework, or LlamaIndex.
Strong command of retrieval techniques including RAG and graph-RAG, vector search, embeddings, reranking, and document-processing pipelines.
Practical context-engineering skills and the ability to consistently improve AI quality through prompt and context design.
Experience with evals and observability as first-class concerns, including evaluation suites, tracing, regression testing, reliability, latency, and cost management.
Familiarity with tooling such as LangSmith, Langfuse, Braintrust, Arize Phoenix, MLflow, DeepEval, or Promptfoo.
A solid foundation in data science fundamentals, data analysis, and statistical thinking.
Strong communication skills in English.




Nice to have




Understanding of pharmaceutical clinical and regulatory documents and workflows.
Awareness of GxP, 21 CFR Part 11, GDPR, HIPAA, and computer system validation.
Experience with semantic computing and document structuring.
Experience deploying secure, compliant applications in regulated cloud environments.
Experience with life science upstream source systems such as Veeva.




This position is offered in Spain and Denmark




Who is BASE life science?




Founded in 2007, BASE life science is a business and technology consulting enterprise working exclusively in the life sciences industry.

With headquarters in Denmark and offices across Europe and the United States, we support clients globally. Ambition is encouraged through ownership and continuous learning
Execution matters — we focus on delivering solutions that work in practice
Team spirit and trust guide collaboration across roles, countries, and disciplines
Openness means your ideas are heard, you are challenged constructively, and supported as you grow

HTML:
As our next AI Engineer, you will contribute to solving meaningful challenges across the life sciences industry. We offer specialised management consulting services, working with top-tier life sciences companies as well as biotech startups — locally and globally. From strategy development to hands-on implementation, we support our clients were impact matters most.



About the role


Generative and agentic AI has moved from demos to production. With this, one of our key focus areas is building products with an agentic AI engine at their core that are reliable, secure, auditable, and good enough to put in front of a medical writer or a regulatory reviewer.


That challenge has two halves:


  • The AI engine: Orchestration, retrieval, context engineering, tool use, guardrails, and the evals and observability that make an agentic system able to solve business-critical tasks in a regulated environment.
  • The product around it: The UI, APIs, services, data layers, integrations, and deployment that turn that engine into robust, secure, scalable software customers depend on.


Today these two halves often pull against each other. We are building a team where they pull together. As an AI Engineer, you will focus on the AI engine itself: designing, building, evaluating, and improving agentic systems that solve real business problems in highly regulated environments.

Your responsibilities include:


  • Design, build, and ship AI-powered tools that help life science teams create, review, and manage critical documents and data.
  • Build features such as grounded retrieval, summarisation, document comparison, question answering, and content classification with traceability back to source.
  • Design agentic workflows capable of handling complex business processes.
  • Build evaluation frameworks and observability capabilities that ensure reliability and quality.
  • Ensure solutions are secure, auditable, and scalable in regulated cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GCP).
  • Collaborate closely with clinical, regulatory, quality, and commercial experts to understand their workflows and implement AI solutions that create measurable value.


You bring:


  • Strong Python skills and solid software engineering fundamentals.
  • Hands-on experience with at least one major cloud (AWS, Azure, or GCP).
  • Deep, hands-on experience with enterprise LLMs and their APIs (e.g. Anthropic/Claude, OpenAI, Google), including tool/function calling, structured outputs, and the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
  • Experience building agentic systems with modern orchestration frameworks such as Pydantic AI, LangGraph, OpenAI Agents SDK, AutoGen / Microsoft Agent Framework, or LlamaIndex.
  • Strong command of retrieval techniques including RAG and graph-RAG, vector search, embeddings, reranking, and document-processing pipelines.
  • Practical context-engineering skills and the ability to consistently improve AI quality through prompt and context design.
  • Experience with evals and observability as first-class concerns, including evaluation suites, tracing, regression testing, reliability, latency, and cost management.
  • Familiarity with tooling such as LangSmith, Langfuse, Braintrust, Arize Phoenix, MLflow, DeepEval, or Promptfoo.
  • A solid foundation in data science fundamentals, data analysis, and statistical thinking.
  • Strong communication skills in English.


Nice to have


  • Understanding of pharmaceutical clinical and regulatory documents and workflows.
  • Awareness of GxP, 21 CFR Part 11, GDPR, HIPAA, and computer system validation.
  • Experience with semantic computing and document structuring.
  • Experience deploying secure, compliant applications in regulated cloud environments.
  • Experience with life science upstream source systems such as Veeva.


This position is offered in Spain and Denmark

Title: Centre of Excellence Lead — Data Science & Machine Learning
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Too Good To Go, we have an ambitious goal: to inspire and empower everyone to fight food waste together.

40% of all food produced in the world is wasted. And that has a huge impact on the health of our planet, with 10% of greenhouse gas emissions coming from food waste.

We're more than an app: we are a certified B Corporation with a mission to empower everyone to take action against food waste, so alongside our marketplace app, we create educational tools, explore new business solutions — such as our Retail Technologies offering — and influence legislation to help reduce food waste.

We're growing fast: Our community of 120 million registered users and 180,000 active partners across 20 countries have together already prevented 500+ million meals from going to waste

Your mission

We are looking for a Centre of Excellence Lead to build and grow the data science and machine learning capability at the heart of our Data and Analytics organisation.

The Centre of Excellence (CoE) brings together our Data Scientists and ML Engineers into a single, centralised team that partners with Product and business domains to solve our most important problems. As its Lead, you will own the team's technical direction, raise the bar on delivery and standards, and develop the people in it while staying close enough to the work to coach hands-on.

As a marketplace, many of our most critical challenges revolve around supply-demand dynamics, user behaviour, and marketplace efficiency. Your team works on problems such as personalisation, pricing, optimisation, and forecasting, helping us move from reactive analysis to proactive, model-driven systems.

Your role


Lead and grow the CoE team of Data Scientists and ML Engineers — owning performance management, coaching, hiring, and the development of each individual
Set and raise technical standards across the team, from problem framing and methodology through to experimentation, productionisation, and impact measurement
Stay hands-on as a player-coach — close enough to the technical work to guide design decisions, review approaches, and help unblock the team
Strengthen the DS↔ML handoff, ensuring models move from prototype to production reliably and that the two disciplines collaborate closely from the start of each initiative
Partner with Product and business leaders to identify high-impact opportunities where data science and ML can drive step-change improvements, and translate them into a focused team roadmap
Represent the CoE to Product, Engineering, and leadership stakeholders, making the case for where and how advanced methods create the most value
Partner with the People team to define the capability framework for the data science and ML domain
Champion the responsible use of AI within the team's ways of working, advocating for best practices and integrating new tools where they add genuine value


Requirements


Solid experience leading technical teams of a similar size (around 5 people), including performance management and developing engineers or scientists
A strong technical foundation in data science or machine learning — you have done the technical work yourself and can credibly coach it
Comfortable across both disciplines: you don't need to have managed both Data Scientists and ML Engineers, but you should have enough fluency in each to set direction and earn the team's trust
Track record of delivering ML or DS work that reached production and created measurable business or product impact
Strong grounding in experimentation (A/B testing), measurement, and impact evaluation
Experience with applied problems such as personalisation/recommendation, optimisation, pricing, or forecasting
A pragmatic, current view on AI its maturity, where it adds value, and how to integrate it responsibly into a team's workflow
Strong stakeholder and communication skills — able to influence Product, Engineering, and business leaders
Comfort operating in a fast-paced, evolving environment with a high degree of ownership
Fluent in English, with excellent written and verbal communication skills


Our values


We win together: Food waste is a big beast to fight. We believe in a #oneteam.
We raise the bar: We always push for more. We work smart, smash barriers and elevate one another.
We keep it simple: Our ambitions are bold but our solutions are simple.
We build a legacy: We're proud of the change we're driving.
We care: We always look out for each other. Caring is also about the way we do business. We do the right thing.


What we have to offer


An opportunity to work in a global social-impact company and certified B Corporation, where you can see a real and tangible impact in your role.
The chance to build and lead a centralised data science and ML capability from an early stage, shaping how advanced methods drive decisions across the company.
Work-life balance is important to us! Focus on the job to be done, not the hours spent — there is no need for overtime.
We trust our employees and encourage an autonomous environment with several opportunities to contribute, develop, and take ownership.
To be part of an international company, with over 1,200+ colleagues across 20 countries on the same important mission.
An informal environment, working alongside 170+ passionate people within Tech & Product.
We celebrate our differences and our successes and have a strong values-driven team culture.
We work in a hybrid set-up (4 days per week in our Copenhagen office) and value in-office work weekly.
We may be able to support you with a working visa in Copenhagen, please be prepared to discuss this topic further at your first-stage interview.


How to apply


We take recruitment very seriously, so please carefully read everything we have written above. Please also check our website and international media to get a good overview of Too Good To Go.
Submit your CV in English.
Please note that we only accept applications coming through our platform. No CV will be accepted by email or LinkedIn direct messaging.


A Movement for Everyone

We want to inspire and empower everyone to fight food waste together. With that mission, it’s only natural that we want to build a diverse and inclusive team of highly capable individuals who are passionate about doing things in a better way. We strongly believe we all excel and are more creative when we’re allowed to be ourselves, and we’re committed to a culture where all of us belong.

We are an equal opportunity employer and all employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit and business need. If you need reasonable accommodation at any point in the application or interview process, please let us know.

Title: Business Intelligence (BI) Developer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
As Our Next Business Intelligence (BI) Developer You Can Play a Key Role in Shaping Our Company Strategy

You will work with diverse modern data platforms and support valuable data-driven decision-making. The role involves responsibility for designing, developing, and optimizing data pipelines while creating insightful reports and dashboards for a variety of stakeholders.

Get To Know Us

The RiskPoint Group is a fast-growing global Managing General Underwriter (MGU), providing specialist insurance solutions across multiple lines of business in close partnership with leading insurance companies.

We are a global company of more than 325 employees, collaborating across borders while leveraging local market expertise in 17 locations. We combine deep underwriting expertise with hands-on, in-house claims handling, and high-quality internal business support, to deliver outstanding service and results to the market.

With us, you are trusted with responsibility. We invest in your development, value initiative, and give you the opportunity to shape your role, grow your expertise, and make a real impact.

About Your Team

You will join our Business Intelligence team, which currently consists of three members across Europe. You will join a small, highly skilled sub-team within our growing global IT function, working within the global Finance and Operations department. Our IT team brings together talented IT professionals across Denmark and the UK, combining strong technical talent with extensive insurance industry experience.

Your colleagues form part of our wider global IT team, operating across 17 countries, with 23 experts internationally.

What Will You Do?

Your work will directly to support data-driven decisions across the entire company and therefore we are looking for someone who is detail-oriented and a proactive problem-solver with strong communication skills.

You will collaborate with colleagues across the entire company and work closely with key stakeholders, including analysts, to ensure a solid understanding of data requirements and delivery of optimized reports and dashboards that provide actionable insights.

Key responsibilities include:


Collaborating with analysts & internal stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver optimized reports
Designing, developing, and maintaining scalable data platforms to support various business needs
Implementing data integrations solutions using ETL tools
Ensuring data quality and integrity across all data platforms
Monitoring and troubleshooting data issues to maintain reliability
Staying up-to-date with the latest industry trends to continuously improve the data architecture and BI capabilities


This position is newly created to support our growth strategy and add further value to the team. You will have the opportunity to bring your experience and insights, whilst learning new skills, and developing your career.

This position will be based in Copenhagen and is office based.

About You

We are looking for someone with a strong technical background and a proven ability to deliver results that support the business, while working effectively with stakeholders across an international organization. You should be a self-starter, who is curious, comfortable working autonomously, and has ambition to drive change. An interest in coaching and developing others is also an advantage.

To be successful in this role, you should have:


A Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field
5 or more years of professional experience in BI development
An excellent understanding of SQL with professional experience on top data platforms (e.g., PostgreSQL, Microsoft SQL, Snowflake)
Experience deploying and managing data solutions on cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud)
Proficiency in at least one programming language; e.g., Python, Go, or Java
Experience creating reports and dashboards using BI tools like Power BI, Terraform, or Tableau
Hands-on experience designing and implementing ETL processes
A strong understanding of data modeling, data warehousing, and database design
A proven track record of working effectively in a team, communicating technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders, and solving complex data problems


Experience with the following is preferred but not required:


Large-scale data processing technologies (e.g., Hadoop, Spark, Iceberg)
Modern data tools like Trino and dbt (data build tool)
Terraform for infrastructure as code
DevOps methodologies and tools (CI/CD pipelines, version control)


You must have current residency in Denmark with the necessary work and residency permits in place before applying.

Spoken and written fluency in English is essential for this role, ideally together with a basic understanding of Danish. Other languages are an advantage.

How Do You Apply?

We are looking forward to receiving your CV and responses to the application questions. Please apply in English.

We wish to receive your application by July 8, 2026.

For more information, please contact Zubair Sabir, CTO via *protected email*.

Please note that we do not accept applications via e-mail. All further communication will be in English.

About The RiskPoint Group

The RiskPoint Group is one of Europe’s largest Managing General Underwriters (MGUs), providing best in class insurance solutions to businesses and their advisors globally. The RiskPoint Group operates as RiskPoint in Europe and Asia and RP Underwriting in the rest of the world.

With 325+ employees and 18 locations in Europe, North America, and Asia Pacific, the RiskPoint Group employs expert underwriting and specialized, in-house claims teams within Accident & Health, Transactional Risk, Property & Construction, Renewable Energy, Offshore Upstream, and Liabilities, including Financial Lines, Cyber, and Casualty.

The RiskPoint Group is a Coverholder with Lloyd’s of London and is backed by a strong panel of well-reputed insurance companies. The RiskPoint Group core values are: trust, openness & honesty, respect & tolerance, drive & dedication as well as being part of a winning team in a competitive environment.
