Title: Senior AI Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Senior AI Engineer




As a Senior AI Engineer, you will design, develop, and deploy advanced AI models and systems that solve complex business challenges. You’ll collaborate with a cross-functional team to translate AI research into scalable, production-ready solutions.




This is a hands-on role where you’ll work on everything from LLM-based, ML or other AI techniques, ensuring our AI initiatives deliver measurable impact.







About the team




We are looking for a Senior AI Engineer to join our AI Team, known as the Growth Catalyst Team, a fast-moving AI unit focused on delivering customer-facing AI features. The team owns the full lifecycle: identifying customer problems, crafting rapid solutions, and bringing them to market in six-month delivery cycles. Structured for speed and innovation, the group operates with exploratory cycles and a strong commercial lens, continually adapting to market opportunities every half-year.




The team is cross‑functional, bringing together product management skills, UX design, and diverse AI, data science, and engineering competencies. The team is focused on customer‑driven discovery, rapid delivery, and the development of AI- and LLM‑centric capabilities through close cross-disciplinary collaboration.







What’s in it for you?




Join a fast-paced, high-impact role at the intersection of Cybersecurity and AI. You’ll work alongside some of the brightest minds in a domain that’s rapidly evolving, where AI enhances defence and threat detection in real time. Expect a dynamic, challenging environment as the race between defenders and attackers accelerates—from cutting-edge research to real-world, production-grade solutions. If you’re passionate about AI and security, this is the place to make a measurable difference, shape the future of cyber defence, and ride the wave of explosive growth in both fields.







Key Responsibilities




Develop and deploy Generative AI solutions, including LLM and SLM fine-tuning and optimisation.
Build retrieval pipelines using vector databases and orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain, SciPy, or PyTorch).
Collaborate with engineering teams and integrate AI into existing products.
Monitor, evaluate, and continuously improve AI systems in production.
Stay current with AI trends and recommend innovative approaches.







Competencies / Requirements




Expected Skills & Qualifications

5+ years of experience in engineering or data science
Proficiency in Python and familiarity with frameworks like SciPy, PyTorch or other data science/machine learning libraries
Hands-on experience with LLMs, context engineering, and fine-tuning techniques
Experienced with building Agentic AI to enable LLM to solve concrete problems
Knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure)
Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to work in a collaborative environment
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field




Nice-to-Have

Experience with deploying SLMs in customer environments, balancing compute needs with other essential services, including optimising the throughput of the SLM
Familiarity with ethical AI principles and bias mitigation
Exposure to cybersecurity or Security Operations-related AI applications







Why you would love working at Guardsix:




We’re on a growth journey, and we offer a challenging and exciting international work environment on the cutting edge of cybersecurity technology. We strive to deliver outstanding results in a positive, collaborative, and inclusive atmosphere with great opportunities for personal growth and development.




At the heart of everything we do is our values: Collaboration, Passion and Impact. These values are not just words but our way of living. By design, we push to create a culture that is dynamic, positive, and contagious. Where each person can show up as their true self, knowing they are valued, heard, and respected.







Get to know Guardsix:




Headquartered in Copenhagen, Denmark, with offices across Europe, the USA, and Asia, Guardsix is a multinational and multicultural cybersecurity company. Senior AI Engineer


As a Senior AI Engineer, you will design, develop, and deploy advanced AI models and systems that solve complex business challenges. You’ll collaborate with a cross-functional team to translate AI research into scalable, production-ready solutions.


This is a hands-on role where you’ll work on everything from LLM-based, ML or other AI techniques, ensuring our AI initiatives deliver measurable impact.



About the team


We are looking for a Senior AI Engineer to join our AI Team, known as the Growth Catalyst Team, a fast-moving AI unit focused on delivering customer-facing AI features. The team owns the full lifecycle: identifying customer problems, crafting rapid solutions, and bringing them to market in six-month delivery cycles. Structured for speed and innovation, the group operates with exploratory cycles and a strong commercial lens, continually adapting to market opportunities every half-year.


The team is cross‑functional, bringing together product management skills, UX design, and diverse AI, data science, and engineering competencies. The team is focused on customer‑driven discovery, rapid delivery, and the development of AI- and LLM‑centric capabilities through close cross-disciplinary collaboration.



What’s in it for you?


Join a fast-paced, high-impact role at the intersection of Cybersecurity and AI. You’ll work alongside some of the brightest minds in a domain that’s rapidly evolving, where AI enhances defence and threat detection in real time. Expect a dynamic, challenging environment as the race between defenders and attackers accelerates—from cutting-edge research to real-world, production-grade solutions. If you’re passionate about AI and security, this is the place to make a measurable difference, shape the future of cyber defence, and ride the wave of explosive growth in both fields.



Key Responsibilities


  • Develop and deploy Generative AI solutions, including LLM and SLM fine-tuning and optimisation.
  • Build retrieval pipelines using vector databases and orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain, SciPy, or PyTorch).
  • Collaborate with engineering teams and integrate AI into existing products.
  • Monitor, evaluate, and continuously improve AI systems in production.
  • Stay current with AI trends and recommend innovative approaches.



Competencies / Requirements


Expected Skills & Qualifications

  • 5+ years of experience in engineering or data science
  • Proficiency in Python and familiarity with frameworks like SciPy, PyTorch or other data science/machine learning libraries
  • Hands-on experience with LLMs, context engineering, and fine-tuning techniques
  • Experienced with building Agentic AI to enable LLM to solve concrete problems
  • Knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure)
  • Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to work in a collaborative environment
  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field


Nice-to-Have

  • Experience with deploying SLMs in customer environments, balancing compute needs with other essential services, including optimising the throughput of the SLM
  • Familiarity with ethical AI principles and bias mitigation
  • Exposure to cybersecurity or Security Operations-related AI applications

Title: Business Intelligence Specialist
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

As the leading Nordic online department store - 1,600+ brands, 14 markets, one of the world's most automated fulfilment centres - data powers every decision we make at Boozt.

We're hiring two new colleagues across our BI function: one in our Logistics Reporting & Analytics (Warehouse & operations) and in our Commercial Business Partner Team.

Different teams, different challenges, one thing in common: your data insights help shape smarter decisions and turn them into real business impact.

Logistics Reporting & Analytics: Early Career


Operations-focused



Learn the business from the ground up. Every order that reaches a customer is the result of an efficient supply chain, a distribution network, and a customer service operation working in sync. You'll sit close to the warehouse floor, fulfilment, returns, and last mile, turning operational data into dashboards and analyses that help teams move faster and smarter.

WHAT YOU WILL DO


Build, develop, and maintain QlikCloud apps end to end - from data load and modelling to the dashboards teams use every day
Write and optimise load scripts, build data models, and keep apps fast and reliable as data volumes grow
Run ad hoc analysis in Qlik to answer operational questions quickly when they come up
Turn requirements from distribution, supply chain, and customer service teams into well-built, reusable reporting
Maintain and improve our existing Qlik reporting - refactoring, fixing, and extending what's already there
Work with our data and engineering teams on data quality and the pipelines that feed Qlik (BigQuery)



YOUR PROFILE


2> years in a BI, analytics data role - internships, thesis work, and student jobs count
Comfortable with SQL and Qlik scripting / data modelling, or genuinely motivated to get there fast
Hands-on with QlikCloud, or able to learn it quickly (experience with Power BI, Tableau, or Looker transfers well; we use Qlik with BigQuery)
You move from "here's the data" to "here's what we should build"
Curious about how the business actually works, not just the numbers it produces



Commercial Business Partner Team: Mid-level


Engineering-focused



Work with large and complex datasets to build the data foundation behind key commercial decisions. You'll design and maintain the data models, pipelines, and BigQuery architecture that our commercial teams rely on to understand performance, set priorities, and identify growth opportunities. Your work powers high-impact commercial decisions.

WHAT YOU WILL DO


Design and maintain the BigQuery data infrastructure powering commercial reporting
Develop Qlik Sense scripts and maintain dashboards and lead our migration to Qlik Cloud
Integrate data from multiple source systems using SQL, including one-off data pulls when needed
Document data models, calculations, and mappings so the work stays transparent and maintainable
Partner with analysts and business partners to turn business requirements into robust data products



YOUR PROFILE

2+< years building BI solutions end to end: data models, pipelines, KPIs, dashboards
Strong SQL and scripting skills, with hands-on experience integrating data from multiple source systems
Proactive and driving, you push work forward without waiting to be told
Comfortable translating non-technical requirements into technical solutions
Bachelor's in computer science, information systems, or a related field; fluent English
Proven Qlik experience is a plus


The process runs over the summer - interviews may first take place in August.

Apply today – we’re excited to learn more about you!

Only applications submitted in English will be considered.

Important: Applicants must have the legal right to work in Denmark at the time of employment. Please note that this position does not include visa sponsorship or relocation support.

We review applications on an ongoing basis. You can read more about the process under Recruitment.

BOOZT PERKS


Great personal and internal career development opportunities
A culture built on our values of trust, freedom, and responsibility
Driven and passionate international colleagues
A generous employee discount
Healthy and delicious breakfast and lunch, top-quality coffee, and fresh fruit available all day
In-house gym, onsite masseuse, and medical doctor
An amazing HQ in central Copenhagen – the iconic, energy-efficient Krystallen building, recognized for its architectural design and sustainability. Enjoy breathtaking city views and much more!

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Middelfart Municipality, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Vil du være med til at bygge stabile og skalerbare data pipelines, der driver datadrevet beslutningstagning i en global organisation? Trives du i et moderne, cloud-first engineering miljø, hvor samarbejde, automation og innovation er i fokus? Så kunne dette være rollen for dig.




Om rollen og teamet

Som Data Engineer bliver du en del af Unit IT, hvor du arbejder i et konsulentdrevet setup, der leverer high-value services på tværs af USTC Group.

Til at starte med vil du indgå i et dedikeret team med fokus på at opbygge en Unified Data Platform for Bunker Holding Group. Her får du en central rolle i at etablere skalerbare, sikre og stabile ingestion capabilities, der gør det muligt at flytte data effektivt fra kildesystemer ind i en stærk og fremtidssikret platform.




Du vil arbejde tæt sammen med integration engineers, platform engineers, architects og stakeholders på tværs af organisationen og bidrage til det fælles mål om at opbygge et moderne og genanvendeligt datafundament, der understøtter både nuværende og fremtidige forretningsbehov.




Dine ansvarsområder

Du vil have en aktiv rolle i at designe, udvikle og drive ingestion capabilities på tværs af den Unified Data Platform.




I rollen arbejder du hands-on med data engineering og sikrer, at data kan ingestes, transformeres og gøres tilgængeligt på en sikker, skalerbar og stabil måde. Du samarbejder tæt med engineers, architects og stakeholders for at etablere stærke ingestion patterns og understøtte et robust platformfundament.




Eksempler på dine opgaver inkluderer:

Design, udvikling og vedligeholdelse af data ingestion pipelines fra kildesystemer til bronze layer i den Unified Data Platform
Opbygning og drift af både batch og streaming ingestion patterns på tværs af strukturerede og semi-strukturerede datakilder
Integration med APIs, databaser, filbaserede systemer og event-driven platforme som Azure Service Bus og Kafka
Implementering af skalerbar ingestion og transformationslogik ved brug af teknologier som Databricks og Microsoft Fabric
Samarbejde med integration engineers, platform engineers og architects om at etablere reusable ingestion frameworks og engineering standards
Udvikling og vedligeholdelse af Infrastructure as Code ved brug af Terraform for at sikre automatiserede og konsistente deployments
Implementering af CI/CD pipelines og automation practices for at forbedre deployment kvalitet og operationel effektivitet
Sikring af data quality, reliability, observability og monitoring på tværs af ingestion pipelines
Understøttelse af governance, metadata, lineage og security i samarbejde med arkitektur- og security teams
Bidrage til skalerbar og kontrolleret self-service onboarding af nye datakilder
Deltage i troubleshooting, operationel support og root cause analysis relateret til ingestion pipelines og platform services
Udforske moderne AI-understøttede development- og ingestion-værktøjer, herunder agent-baserede og MCP-enabled patterns, hvor det er relevant




I praksis er dette en rolle, hvor du kombinerer moderne data engineering, platform mindset og automation for at opbygge et skalerbart og fremtidssikret datafundament for organisationen.




Dine kvalifikationer

Vi forventer, at du er en teknisk stærk og samarbejdsorienteret engineer, som trives med at løse komplekse data ingestion og integrationsopgaver.




Du har sandsynligvis erfaring med:

Moderne data engineering og data ingestion practices
Opbygning af batch og streaming data pipelines
Databricks og/eller Microsoft Fabric
Integration af data fra APIs, databaser, messaging systemer og filbaserede kilder
Event-driven og messaging teknologier som Azure Service Bus og/eller Kafka
Infrastructure as Code, gerne Terraform
Opbygning og vedligeholdelse af CI/CD pipelines og deployment automation
Data quality, monitoring, observability og operational support practices
Cloud-native dataplatforme og distribuerede datasystemer
Stærke samarbejds- og kommunikationsevner på tværs af engineering og forretning




Du arbejder struktureret og kvalitetsbevidst, tager ejerskab for dine leverancer og samarbejder effektivt med både engineers og forretningsmæssige stakeholders.




Det er en fordel, hvis du også har:

Erfaring med medallion architecture og bronze/silver/gold data layering
Erfaring med hybrid dataplatforme
Kendskab til DataOps, DevOps eller platform engineering practices
Erfaring med MCP eller AI-assisted development og automation tooling
Erfaring fra enterprise-scale data platform miljøer




Hvad du kan forvente

En central rolle i opbygningen og videreudviklingen af en moderne, enterprise-grade Unified Data Platform
Høj grad af ejerskab og indflydelse på ingestion standards, engineering practices og platform capabilities
Tæt samarbejde med architects, engineers og forretningen på tværs af organisationen
Et moderne engineering miljø med fokus på automation, skalerbarhed og cloud-native teknologier
Mulighed for at arbejde med nye teknologier inden for data, AI og platform engineering
Et samarbejdssetup, hvor interne og eksterne kollegaer arbejder som ét samlet team




Bliv en del af teamet

Vi ønsker at besætte stillingen hurtigst muligt, så hvis du kan se dig selv i rollen, ser vi frem til at modtage din ansøgning snarest muligt.

Vi behandler ansøgninger og CV’er løbende. Har du spørgsmål, er du velkommen til at kontakte Unit Director, Data Kristian Brendstrup på +45 60 37 67 49.




Om Unit IT

Hos Unit IT bliver du en del af en familieejet virksomhed med en stærk kultur og et samarbejdsorienteret miljø, hvor vi deler viden, hjælper hinanden og skaber værdi sammen. Vores kerneværdier – WE DARE, WE CARE, WE ARE UNITED – er fundamentet for alt, hvad vi gør.




I dag har Unit IT seks lokationer i Danmark med hovedkontor i Middelfart samt et kontor i Prag, Tjekkiet. Unit IT leverer en bred vifte af avancerede services inden for infrastruktur samt private og public cloud løsninger samt specialistviden inden for Cloud, Business Intelligence, Data Platform og Cyber Security.




Unit IT er en del af den danske, familieejede koncern United Shipping & Trading Company (USTC). USTC’s portefølje omfatter aktiviteter inden for oil & energy, shipping, door-to-door logistics, risk management, automotive, IT og sustainable energy og er repræsenteret i 38 lande med mere end 4.500 medarbejdere.

Title: Python Developer (PySpark & AWS Data Engineering) – Copenhagen
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Twoday is the leading digital transformation partner in Northern Europe with a global presence. With approximately 3,000 employees, we collaborate with the most admired private and public organizations to deliver cutting-edge digital solutions. Our deep industry expertise spans Data & AI, software development, digital experiences, and business applications. Operating across the Nordics and Lithuania, our team generated a revenue of 280 million euros in 2023. We serve over 8,000 customers, supporting their digital transformation journeys.

Join Twoday and help build business-critical data solutions for one of Denmark's largest public sector organisations

At Twoday, we help organisations create lasting value through technology. We are looking for an experienced Python Developer to join our Digital Engineering team in a permanent role. You will initially be assigned to a long-term customer project in Lyngby, working as part of a dedicated onsite team delivering and maintaining business-critical data integration and analytics solutions.

As a Twoday consultant, you will be employed by Twoday on a permanent basis. Following the initial assignment, you will have the opportunity to work on other exciting customer engagements across Copenhagen.

The role

You will become part of a team responsible for operating, maintaining and further developing a number of data processing components that provide critical business data to BI tools and user interfaces used for planning and operational management.

The Technology Stack Primarily Consists Of


Python
PySpark
AWS Glue
AWS Step Functions
Athena SQL
GitHub


You will work closely with analysts, architects, developers and infrastructure specialists to design, develop and maintain robust, scalable and high-quality data solutions.

Your Responsibilities

Your responsibilities will include:


Collaborating with the Value Team Lead and Business Analyst to ensure Jira issues contain sufficient information to support efficient and high-quality delivery
Participating in backlog refinement and translating business requirements into robust technical solution designs
Working with domain architects to validate and document technical solution designs
Developing, maintaining and optimising PySpark code, AWS Glue jobs and Athena SQL scripts
Promoting and applying best practices for PySpark and distributed data processing across the development team
Designing and implementing unit tests in accordance with development standards
Implementing data validation, error handling, logging and monitoring solutions, including Soda Core where applicable
Producing and maintaining technical documentation and operational procedures
Performing developer testing, participating in peer reviews and supporting the release process
Creating and executing relevant test cases using Jira and Xray where required
Documenting test results and supporting defect resolution
Collaborating with infrastructure specialists on AWS platform maintenance and optimisation, including services such as S3, EC2, IAM, CloudWatch, Terraform and Jenkins
Supporting knowledge sharing and ensuring effective handover of technical knowledge within the team


What We're Looking For

We are looking for someone who brings solid technical expertise together with a collaborative mindset.

To Succeed In This Role, You Should Have


At least five years of relevant full-time professional experience developing Python-based data solutions using technologies such as PySpark, SQL and AWS
Commercial experience with PySpark and distributed data processing
AWS Glue and the wider AWS ecosystem
SQL, preferably Athena SQL
ETL development and data engineering best practices
Git and modern version control workflows
Unit testing and automated quality assurance
Technical documentation and structured development processes
Agile delivery methods and Jira


Experience with one or more of the following would be an advantage:


AWS Step Functions
Soda Core
CloudWatch
Terraform
Jenkins
Xray test management


You enjoy working closely with business stakeholders and technical colleagues and take pride in delivering maintainable, high-quality solutions.

Mandatory Requirements

To be considered for this role, you must:


Be fluent in both spoken and written Danish
Be able to work full-time onsite in Lyngby during the initial customer assignment
Be willing to work onsite at customer locations across Copenhagen on future assignments, as customer requirements may vary and can include working onsite five days per week


Please note that while the initial assignment offers the possibility of working from home on Wednesdays, this arrangement is specific to the first customer engagement and cannot be guaranteed on future assignments.

Title: Data Management Consultant
Description:
Job Description

Are you passionate about data and driven by the opportunity to make a real impact by helping companies turn facts into actionable insights? Do you thrive in dynamic environments where you work with some of the most complex and modern data solutions? If so, you might be our next colleague.

Responsibilities & Technologies

In Data Management, you will become part of a team that contributes to enabling our customers to develop and manage their business based on data and knowledge.

You will work in the intersection of technology and business. Information and data are at the core of our work, and we can see that our analyses, advice, and final solutions create value for our customers. The work involves developing technical solutions to collect, process, model, analyze, and visualize data.

To provide the best possible advice and support to our customers, we place great importance on staying up to date with the latest trends and technologies in data management. You will work with a broad range of market-leading technologies, including: Azure SQL Server, Snowflake, dbt, Microsoft SQL Server, and others.

Your tasks are highly varied and depend on your skills and the direction you wish to develop.

Your Responsibilites Will Typically Include


Design, modeling, and building of data structures such as databases.
Structuring and collection of data from various sources.
Implementation of business logic on data for use in reporting.
Customer advisory, strategy work, and business analysis in collaboration with our customers.
Development of reports for business users with a focus on front-end.


About You

You are newly graduated or have up to a couple of years of relevant professional experience within data management. Through your education, you have gained a solid professional foundation and a logical approach to handling complex data and challenges. You thrive in a development-oriented environment with a focus on collaboration and continuous personal and professional development.

To succeed in this role, you have experience with or the interest to learn:


ETL and Database technologies.
Languages such as various versions of SQL, Python, and R.
Business Intelligence tools: Power BI, Tableau, and others.
Full professional proficiency in Danish and English (written and spoken) is a must.


Continue developing with us

At Netcompany, we focus on your personal and professional development.

When you join us, you will be assigned a personal mentor who will guide you through your development and help you achieve your goals. The close relationship with an experienced colleague helps ensure the well-being of each employee and provides you with concrete insights into the many different career opportunities as you build your experience.

Additionally, you will be enrolled in our internal training programme, Netcompany Academy, where you are offered courses and seminars on everything from technologies to customer management. All courses are taught by experienced Netcompany colleagues, ensuring that all content is highly relevant and builds upon your professional skills, preparing you for various tasks and continuously improving your expertise throughout your career with us.

Join an inclusive community

At Netcompany, we highly prioritise an inclusive community. We actively promote an open and welcoming work culture based on collaboration and mutual trust, where there is always an opportunity for sparring.

As part of our culture and values, all employees, regardless of background, identity, and experience, are met with understanding and respect and given equal opportunities for development and well-being.

As we are an international company with offices in many countries, you will have the opportunity to learn and collaborate internationally with colleagues from across the world. We offer a shared environment where we engage with each other across departments and projects. Additionally, we have a wide range of social and professional events that provide you with the best opportunities to expand and build a network early in your career.

Send us your application

Upload your resume and a motivational letter to our recruitment system via the link. If you have unanswered questions, feel free to write to hr.dk@netcompany.com.

All qualified candidates are encouraged to apply regardless of gender, gender expression, sexuality, disability, age, religion and belief, political views, nationality, ethnicity, or social background.

We are committed to creating an inclusive and diverse environment. Our recruitment processes are based on individual merit. If you require any reasonable adjustments or additional support during the interview process, please email our recruiters at reasonableadjustments@netcompany.com for assistance.

Title: Senior Network Analytics Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Maersk connects people, businesses and markets across the world. You will join a team that builds and strengthens the analytical and optimization foundation behind our global network planning. We use data, modelling and advanced optimization algorithms to support better operational and strategic decisions. We are a diverse, international community where different backgrounds, perspectives and experiences make us stronger. We work openly and collaboratively and value curiosity, clarity and shared ownership.

Ready to shape the next generation of Maersk’s optimization intelligence?

This senior role sets direction for how optimisation and modelling guide planning and network design at Maersk. You will define problem structures, design optimisation models and heuristics, and lead new capabilities from concept through validation and into implementation. You will drive the innovation agenda, guide change across the value chain and ensure new methods translate into real improvements for planners and decision makers. You will act as a technical lead, mentor others and help turn strong ideas into scalable, reliable solutions. You will work on challenging container flow, routing and network design problems that require strong problem framing, deep modelling expertise and a practical engineering mindset.

What We Offer

To work at Maersk is to step into a world of opportunity and impact. You’ll collaborate with talented professionals who keep global trade moving—shaping the flow of goods, ideas, and possibilities across the planet. Here, your growth truly matters. With access to world‑class learning, supportive leaders, and space to stretch your potential, you can build a career that takes you further than you imagined. You’ll be part of a diverse, inclusive community where you’re valued for your unique strengths and celebrated for the contributions you make. And with flexible hybrid working and industry‑leading benefits, you’ll have the freedom and support to thrive—professionally and personally

Within your impact and responsibilities, you will:


Design advanced optimisation models and heuristics and take them from idea to validated and implemented capability
Frame complex planning problems as clear mathematical formulations with explicit assumptions and success criteria
Build reusable modelling components that follow modern engineering standards and integrate with production data systems
Improve existing optimisation logic by closing gaps and lifting performance and scalability
Run structured experiments to compare alternatives and verify model behaviour with data
Translate findings into clear recommendations and partner with stakeholders to drive implementation and adoption
Shape the innovation roadmap by identifying new application areas and accelerating promising concepts into pilots and scale
Coach junior analysts and raise standards in modelling practice, documentation and code quality
Communicate results, trade offs and limitations in simple and decision-oriented language


What you'll be accountable for:


Integrity, stability and performance of optimisation logic used in planning and decision making
Robust validation and successful implementation of new modelling features with clear readiness criteria
Clear documentation of assumptions, constraints and data dependencies to support transparent decisions
A value based and well prioritised modelling backlog focused on measurable business impact and time to value
Demonstrated improvements in modelling logic, scalability and performance in real planning scenarios
Effective adoption and correct use of optimisation capabilities across the user community with visible change outcomes
Active contribution to the optimisation innovation pipeline by shaping new concepts, evaluating emerging methods and advancing promising ideas toward pilots and scale
Leadership in the transformation agenda by helping teams shift from manual work to model driven planning and ensuring new solutions are embedded and used with confidence


What you bring:

A deep technical expertise, strong analytical judgement and a record of leading optimisation solutions from first principles through implementation and adoption. You communicate clearly with both technical and non technical audiences and you take ownership of delivery and outcomes.

You offer:


A master’s degree or PhD in engineering, mathematics, computer science or a related quantitative field with exposure to optimisation or modelling
Documented experience designing and deploying optimisation models or decision support algorithms in operational or production environments
Expert level Python skills, experience applying C++ for performance critical components and strong SQL capabilities
Advanced knowledge of Operations Research, including Mixed Integer Programming, graph theory, decomposition methods, and large-scale optimisation
Strength in problem framing, algorithm design, empirical validation and scaling solutions
Confident use of Git and modern software engineering workflows
Ability to collaborate smoothly across diverse functions and to build strong working relationships that support clear communication, shared problem solving and aligned decision making
Ability to lead change by guiding stakeholders through adoption, explaining trade offs and ensuring appropriate use of models
A disciplined approach to documentation, testing, version control and reproducibility


It is a plus if you also bring:


Familiarity with GPU based acceleration or parallel computation for optimising large problem instances
Experience with AI or machine learning methods
Knowledge of deep learning architectures and training workflows
Background in advanced statistics or statistical inference
Experience with time series modelling or forecasting techniques


If you're ready to jump in, push the boundaries of modelling and optimisation, and make a visible impact from day one, let’s connect. We’re excited to see the ideas, expertise, and momentum you’ll bring as we elevate Maersk’s decision‑support capabilities and shape the future of our network planning technologies.

Maersk is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace, and we embrace different styles of thinking. Maersk is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applicants without regard to race, colour, gender, sex, age, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, marital status, sexual orientation, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy or parental leave, veteran status, gender identity, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We will consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with all legal requirements.

We are happy to support your need for any adjustments during the application and hiring process. If you need special assistance or an accommodation to use our website, apply for a position, or to perform a job, please contact us by emailing accommodationrequests@maersk.com.

Title: Senior AI Engineer
Place: Aarhus Municipality, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Aarhus, Denmark

Are you an experienced AI/ML engineer who wants to help us shape how we build, test and operate AI? We're a strong software organization looking for someone to mature our AI practice across our products, while a talented team grows around you.

Stakeholder Intelligence is a unit within Rambøll, with SaaS products including SurveyXact, PeopleXact and Heart&Mind. These products help organizations across the Nordics and Europe understand and improve the lives of the people who matter most to them, whether customers, patients, citizens, employees or children. For some, that means listening to stakeholders and turning their feedback into insight they can act on. For others, it means giving managers what they need to improve the working lives of their teams, or supporting practitioners in daycare and schools in their work on quality and children's development.

We work with sensitive data, and keeping it safe shapes how we build: we primarily run models on our own GPU servers, on-prem. We're expanding our SaaS portfolio with AI-driven value creation, and we're looking for a senior engineer to help us do it responsibly and at scale.

Where you fit in

We're early in our AI journey. We have a mature software organization (50 developers and designers) with deep strength in traditional software engineering, and a Platform Engineering team that builds the shared platform our product teams build and run on. But ML and LLM work is new to us, and our practices for evaluating, testing and operating these systems are still forming. We've recently begun building a dedicated AI Engineering function; it's still small, and much of its work happens alongside Platform Engineering.

AI Engineering at Stakeholder Intelligence is a shared craft. Our AI Engineers are talented but early in their careers. Each is primarily attached to a product team and works closely with that team, while spending a significant portion of their time on shared methods and tooling across products. As our senior engineer, you'll spend even more of your time working broadly across products, and your job is to shape that craft.

You won't own product direction; that stays with the product teams and their Tech Leads. What you'll shape is how we do ML/LLM engineering: the methods, standards and practices for how we develop, test, evaluate, deploy and operate the AI parts of our products. You'll provide direction, bring proven experience, and represent AI engineering in cross-functional discussions with Product Managers, Tech Leads and other senior voices.

This is a hands-on, individual-contributor role with real influence, scoped to the experience you bring.

What You'll Do



Define our evaluation practice: how we determine whether one model, prompt or system genuinely outperforms another, drawing on methods such as golden datasets, deterministic checks, reference-based metrics, LLM-as-a-judge and human evaluation. This is central to the role.
Establish our MLOps foundations and optimize how we serve our on-prem, open-source LLMs, working closely with Platform Engineering: deployment pipelines, model and prompt versioning, evaluation harnesses, monitoring (latency, accuracy, drift), reproducibility, and the shared tooling and standards that help good practice spread across teams.
Build production-grade LLM features hands-on, in close collaboration with Product Managers, Designers, Tech Leads and Software Engineers.
Mentor our AI Engineers and set the patterns and best practices that define how we do AI across the team.
Engage with subject-matter experts and customer-facing teams to ensure AI is used in ways that are meaningful, secure and scalable.



What We're Looking For (must-haves)



Several years of hands-on experience building and operating ML/LLM systems in production, ideally in a SaaS or product context.
Several years working with evaluation and MLOps frameworks, enough to have real opinions about how to evaluate LLM systems: golden datasets, deterministic and reference-based tests, LLM-as-a-judge and its pitfalls, and regression analysis when comparing models.
Solid MLOps practice: deployment, lifecycle management and monitoring in production.
Deep, hands-on experience taking prompt engineering and RAG well beyond proof-of concept into systems that perform reliably in production.
Hands-on experience with AI agents, including their failure modes and a realistic sense of when they're the right tool.
Proficiency in Python (Java a plus).
Strong interpersonal skills and a genuine mentoring mindset. You share direction and experience generously, and you lift the people around you without taking over.
Comfort building foundations in a low-maturity environment.
A strong commitment to data security, guardrails and responsible, ethical AI.



Strong advantages



A track record of establishing ML/LLM practice from scratch (evaluation frameworks, testing discipline, MLOps foundations), rather than only working within practices someone else built. Ideally across more than one team or product.
Hands-on experience running and serving models locally on your own GPU infrastructure, not just using open-source models through a hosted API.
Experience fine-tuning open-source LLMs to make them more domain-specific.
Experience translating emerging research into practical applications.



What We Offer



A foundation-owned company with a clear purpose to create a better world.
Competitive salary.
Support for your personal and professional development from day one, in an environment of professional exchange and learning among peers.
A hybrid working model balancing on-site presence with remote flexibility.
Health insurance and pension scheme.
A sixth week of holiday.
A strong social community with regular social events in a dynamic environment.
An attractive lunch scheme in perhaps Aarhus' best canteen.



How To Apply

Apply online with your CV and a cover letter. We expect you to understand, speak and write Danish (minimum CEFR B1, preferably B2 or higher) and to work on-site in Aarhus at least 3 days a week.

We'll be conducting interviews in the second half of August and through September, so please send your application before then to be sure it's considered. Reach out to our Head of Software Engineering, Rasmus Hallenberg-Larsen, at rah@ramboll.com if you'd like to know more about the role

We have a mature software organization (50 developers and designers) with deep strength in traditional software engineering, and a Platform Engineering team that builds the shared platform our product teams build and run on. But ML and LLM work is new to us, and our practices for evaluating, testing and operating these systems are still forming. We've recently begun building a dedicated AI Engineering function; it's still small, and much of its work happens alongside Platform Engineering.

AI Engineering at Stakeholder Intelligence is a shared craft. Our AI Engineers are talented but early in their careers. Each is primarily attached to a product team and works closely with that team, while spending a significant portion of their time on shared methods and tooling across products. As our senior engineer, you'll spend even more of your time working broadly across products, and your job is to shape that craft.

You won't own product direction; that stays with the product teams and their Tech Leads. What you'll shape is how we do ML/LLM engineering: the methods, standards and practices for how we develop, test, evaluate, deploy and operate the AI parts of our products. You'll provide direction, bring proven experience, and represent AI engineering in cross-functional discussions with Product Managers, Tech Leads and other senior voices.

This is a hands-on, individual-contributor role with real influence, scoped to the experience you bring.

What You'll Do


  • Define our evaluation practice: how we determine whether one model, prompt or system genuinely outperforms another, drawing on methods such as golden datasets, deterministic checks, reference-based metrics, LLM-as-a-judge and human evaluation. This is central to the role.
  • Establish our MLOps foundations and optimize how we serve our on-prem, open-source LLMs, working closely with Platform Engineering: deployment pipelines, model and prompt versioning, evaluation harnesses, monitoring (latency, accuracy, drift), reproducibility, and the shared tooling and standards that help good practice spread across teams.
  • Build production-grade LLM features hands-on, in close collaboration with Product Managers, Designers, Tech Leads and Software Engineers.
  • Mentor our AI Engineers and set the patterns and best practices that define how we do AI across the team.
  • Engage with subject-matter experts and customer-facing teams to ensure AI is used in ways that are meaningful, secure and scalable.


What We're Looking For (must-haves)


  • Several years of hands-on experience building and operating ML/LLM systems in production, ideally in a SaaS or product context.
  • Several years working with evaluation and MLOps frameworks, enough to have real opinions about how to evaluate LLM systems: golden datasets, deterministic and reference-based tests, LLM-as-a-judge and its pitfalls, and regression analysis when comparing models.
  • Solid MLOps practice: deployment, lifecycle management and monitoring in production.
  • Deep, hands-on experience taking prompt engineering and RAG well beyond proof-of concept into systems that perform reliably in production.
  • Hands-on experience with AI agents, including their failure modes and a realistic sense of when they're the right tool.
  • Proficiency in Python (Java a plus).
  • Strong interpersonal skills and a genuine mentoring mindset. You share direction and experience generously, and you lift the people around you without taking over.
  • Comfort building foundations in a low-maturity environment.
  • A strong commitment to data security, guardrails and responsible, ethical AI.


Strong advantages


  • A track record of establishing ML/LLM practice from scratch (evaluation frameworks, testing discipline, MLOps foundations), rather than only working within practices someone else built. Ideally across more than one team or product.
  • Hands-on experience running and serving models locally on your own GPU infrastructure, not just using open-source models through a hosted API.
  • Experience fine-tuning open-source LLMs to make them more domain-specific.
  • Experience translating emerging research into practical applications.


What We Offer


  • A foundation-owned company with a clear purpose to create a better world.
  • Competitive salary.
  • Support for your personal and professional development from day one, in an environment of professional exchange and learning among peers.
  • A hybrid working model balancing on-site presence with remote flexibility.
  • Health insurance and pension scheme.
  • A sixth week of holiday.
  • A strong social community with regular social events in a dynamic environment.
  • An attractive lunch scheme in perhaps Aarhus' best canteen.


How To Apply

Apply online with your CV and a cover letter. We expect you to understand, speak and write Danish (minimum CEFR B1, preferably B2 or higher) and to work on-site in Aarhus at least 3 days a week.

We'll be conducting interviews in the second half of August and through September, so please send your application before then to be sure it's considered. Reach out to our Head of Software Engineering, Rasmus Hallenberg-Larsen, at rah@ramboll.com if you'd like to know more about the role

Title: REVENUE & ANALYTICS MANAGER
Place: Kastrup, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
CARGO REVENUE AND ANALYTICS MANAGER

Join SAS Cargo and help shape the future of our performance.

SAS Cargo is a premium multi-market airline driving sustainable profits on our way towards a consolidated future. SAS Cargo’s services are based on the cargo capacity of SAS network, extended by dedicated truck operations. Besides expanding our presence in main hub Copenhagen, we are transforming how we make commercial decisions by using more data, acting faster, and serve better.

If you’re passionate about analytics, commercial optimization, and creating measurable business impact, this is your opportunity.

You will be part of Commercial’s RM and Pricing team, a small and diverse team responsible for optimizing SAS Cargo’s revenue and contribution, and ensuring that SAS Cargo is offering a competitive network, relevant products and competitive rates on SAS capacity.

What You’ll Do

In-depth analysis


Analyze performance across yields, load factors, revenue drivers, and flights to identify trends and improvement opportunities
Conduct scenario modelling and forecasting together with the Finance Business Controller and Partner and Strategic Pricing Manager to support strategic decisions and reporting outlooks
Contribute to our ongoing ML/AI initiatives, enhancing our predictive capabilities and enabling smarter, data‑driven decision‑making.
Support or lead development of business cases and business follow-up across SAS Cargo
Act as an internal ambassador for data-driven decision making within SAS Cargo.


Revenue Management


Optimize revenue contribution on SAS belly capacity on your own route portfolio through optimal rates, overbooking decisions and sales mix analysis
Work cross functional with Operations managers and planners, and Sales on day-to-day route optimization
Monitor and adjust steering (rates, capacity allocation) based on market changes and input from stakeholders
Evaluate and set up allotment allocations
Respond to special rate requests (spot market)
Work with Global Sales and Strategic Pricing Manager to set rates correctly and win deals
Work with our RM architect and rest of the RM-team to continuously develop best practice and adoption of our upcoming new RM system enhancements.


What You Bring


Required: 1-2 years of experience in an analytical or technical role. Minimum BSc degree or equivalent. Previous experience in revenue management, data/business analysis, or a related field within aviation, logistics, transportation, hospitality or other network industries is big plus.
Preferred: Experience with statistical modeling, RM systems, programming, or quantitative analytics will make you stand out.


Your strengths


Strategic & Analytical Thinking: You thrive on solving complex problems, making data-driven decisions, and turning insights into strategies that drive business growth.
Clear Communication: You excel at explaining complex data insights in a way that’s both clear and compelling, connecting with colleagues across departments.
Proactive & Results-Driven: You believe in making things happen! You take the initiative, move quickly from analysis to action, and consistently deliver impactful results.
Why You Should Apply: At our company, we believe in supporting each other and championing everyone’s potential. If you're passionate about what you do and want to work in a place that values personal development, well-being, and empowerment, this is the role for you.


Our Culture At SAS Cargo And What We Offer

At SAS Cargo, you will join a collaborative and supportive team where you can take initiative, influence key commercial processes, and contribute to creating value across the organization.

We Offer


A role with real responsibility and opportunity to drive improvements in revenue and decision-making
A strong team culture where we collaborate across SAS Cargo and the wider SAS organization, recognize each other’s efforts, and share successes
A structured onboarding program with support from experienced colleagues and your manager
Workplace flexibility, including the possibility of some remote work and option to access a gym
Social activities with colleagues in and outside of work
Access to staff tickets and discounts. You can read more at https://www.sasgroup.net/career/working-at-sas/benefits-and-compensations/


Additional Information 


Interviews will not take place before 2nd half of August. Application deadline 16 august 2026. Note that we cannot handle any applications that are sent by email, due to GDPR regulations.
Desired start date: As soon as possible.
Position: Fulltime (100%) starting with a 3-month probation period
This position is based at SAS Cargo’s new HQ facility at Copenhagen airport, and you will be expected to work onsite. Some remote work may also be an option, depending on the agreement made with your manager.
To be eligible for this position you need an EU-work permit. Please note that we ask you to inform us if you currently have a valid EU-work permit.


SAS Cargo is the leading provider of air freight solutions to, from and within Scandinavia, delivering world class quality and customer care. SAS Cargo’s services are based on the cargo capacity of SAS network, extended by dedicated truck operations.

Title: Data Integration Engineer – skab stabile og effektive dataintegrationer til kritisk infrastruktur
Place: Fredericia, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Vil du arbejde med avancerede integrationer og dataflows, der understøtter Danmarks energisystem? Hos Energinet bliver du en central del af den digitale udvikling og bidrager direkte til den grønne omstilling gennem stabile og effektive integrationsløsninger.

Bliv en del af Data Engineering og Enablement

Du bliver en del af et team, hvor vi udvikler og vedligeholder integrationer, der understøtter Energinets forretningskritiske systemer. Vi arbejder tæt sammen med både forretning og IT og OT for at sikre korrekt dataudveksling, høj datakvalitet og stabile platforme.

Hos os møder du et team, der prioriterer samarbejde, faglig sparring og løbende forbedringer højt – og hvor du får mulighed for at bidrage til både løsninger og arbejdsmetoder.

Du udvikler integrationer og sikrer stabile dataflows

I jobbet får du en central rolle i både udvikling og drift. Du arbejder med moderne integrationsteknologier og bidrager til automatisering og optimering.

Du kommer bl.a. til at:


udvikle og vedligeholde systemintegrationer (API, REST, SOAP, SQL, ETL)
udvikle og optimere data ingestion pipelines, så dataflows er stabile og effektive
sikre performance og drift på integrationsplatforme
automatisere processer og reducere manuelle arbejdsgange via dataopsamling
samarbejde med interne teams om korrekt dataudveksling og compliance



”Du får en central rolle i at sikre stabile integrationer, som hele organisationen er afhængig af. Samtidig arbejder vi tæt sammen og hjælper hinanden med at finde de bedste løsninger,” udtaler Kenneth Eckstrøm, Manager.

Du har erfaring med dataintegration og arbejder struktureret

Vi forestiller os, at du har en teknisk baggrund og erfaring med integrationsløsninger – og at du trives med komplekse problemstillinger.

Vi lægger især vægt på, at du:


har praktisk erfaring med integrationsteknologier og API-udvikling i lukkede netværk
har kendskab til ETL-processer, databaser (SQL) og integrationsplatforme
forstår data governance og kvalitetssikring
arbejder analytisk og struktureret med tekniske udfordringer
kommunikerer klart og samarbejder godt med både tekniske og forretningsmæssige profiler



Har du certificeringer inden for fx Azure, MuleSoft eller Databricks, er det en fordel – men ikke et krav. Du har minimum ca. 4 års erfaring i tilsvarende eller sammenlignelig rolle.

”Vi arbejder med komplekse systemlandskaber, og vi kommer i mål, fordi vi deler viden og løfter opgaverne i fællesskab.” – Kollega i teamet

Vil du være med til at gøre en forskel sammen med os?

Så ser vi frem til at modtage dit motiverede CV senest 9. august. Sæt allerede nu et par forsigtige kryds i kalenderen – vi forventer at holde 1. samtale online 17. august og 2. samtale i Fredericia 24. august.

Er du nysgerrig på din kommende leder, eller har du spørgsmål til jobbet, kan du se nærmere på LinkedIn eller kontakte Manager, Kenneth Eckstrøm på tlf. +45 2333 8984.

Særlige forhold: Da Energinet løser samfundskritiske opgaver, udfører vi baggrundstjek forud for ansættelsen. Det er en forudsætning for ansættelse, at dit baggrundstjek er foreneligt med ansættelse i Energinet. Baggrundstjekket gennemføres desuden én gang årligt.

Jobbet kræver desuden, at du kan opnå og opretholde sikkerhedsgodkendelse. På PET's hjemmeside kan du læse mere om sikkerhedsgodkendelse.

Vi ser mangfoldighed som en styrke og opfordrer alle kvalificerede, uanset baggrund, til at søge jobbet. Vi arbejder aktivt og målrettet med mangfoldighed i Energinet og opfordrer dig derfor til at udelade billede, oplysninger om alder, civilstand og lignende, for at minimere ubevidste biases i processen. Du kan læse mere om vores mangfoldighedspolitik her.

Du bliver en del af Energinet Eltransmission A/S, der har til opgave at udvikle, bygge, eje og drive det nødvendige eltransmissionsnet, Herefter stilles det til rådighed for samfundet med den rette kapacitet, i den rette kvalitet, så omkostningseffektivt som muligt og således, at anlægget kan udnyttes optimalt og sikkert. Eltransmission sikrer optimal Asset Management ved at have fokus på anlæggenes samlede levetid - fra vugge til grav.

Glæd dig til at blive en del af en organisation med et meningsfuldt formål, hvor vi forsyner Danmark med el og gas, og omstiller energisystemet til grøn energi.

For at lykkes med den opgave, skal vi have dygtige og engagerede medarbejdere. Derfor har vi skabt rammer, hvor udvikling prioriteres, nye idéer bliver taget alvorligt, og der er balance mellem arbejde og privatliv. Vi kalder det Energi til mere, og du kan blive endnu klogere på det her

Title: AI Engineer / Fullstack developer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
YouSee søger AI Engineer / Fullstack Developer (Internal AI Products)

Vil du være med til at bygge AI produkter, der ikke bare lyder smarte i en PowerPoint, men faktisk skaber værdi i hverdagen?

Vi søger en AI Agentic Engineer, der vil være med til at udvikle AI-agenter og agentic workflows, som kan hjælpe forretningen med at løse konkrete opgaver smartere, hurtigere og mere ensartet.

Du behøver ikke kunne alt fra dag ét. Det vigtigste er, at du har en stærk teknisk grundforståelse, er nysgerrig og har lyst til at lære, teste, bygge og justere, når virkeligheden viser sig at være lidt mere besværlig end demoen.

Om rollen

Du bliver en central del af Internal AI Products, hvor vi udvikler og skalerer AI‑løsninger til YouSee’s ansatte med fokus på at gøre arbejdsprocesser hurtigere, nemmere og mere konsistente på tværs af systemer og funktioner.

Dit fokus er at bygge løsninger, som understøtter konkrete workflows, automatiserer opgaver og integrerer tæt med den måde medarbejdere allerede arbejder på.

Du kommer bl.a. til at arbejde med:


Designe og udvikle AI‑agenter samt agentic workflows
Bygge løsninger med LLMs, RAG, API’er og interne systemer
Udvikling af end‑to‑end AI‑løsninger (fra idé og prototype til produktion)
Integration af AI‑løsninger i eksisterende platforme og arbejdsgange
Arbejde med orchestration, routing, prompts og context handling på tværs af systemer
Tæt samarbejde med product managers, data scientists, business analysts og forretningen
Løbende optimering af løsninger baseret på feedback og faktisk brug i organisationen



Din profil

Vi forestiller os, at du:


Har erfaring med frontend og/eller backend‑udvikling og er komfortabel med at arbejde end‑to‑end
Har arbejdet med API‑integrationer og kan bygge løsninger, der spiller sammen med eksisterende systemer
Har erfaring med - eller stærk interesse for - generativ AI, LLMs eller AI‑baserede applikationer
Har kendskab til RAG, embeddings og/eller vector databases
Har erfaring med cloud‑platforme som Azure eller AWS
Har forståelse for, hvordan AI‑løsninger designes, bygges og bringes i produktion
Kan omsætte komplekse behov til konkrete, skalerbare løsninger
Arbejder struktureret og trives med teknisk ansvar
Har en nysgerrig og eksperimenterende tilgang til ny teknologi



Du behøver ikke sætte flueben ved det hele. Hvis du kan se dig selv i flere af punkterne og har lyst til at lære resten, så vil vi rigtig gerne høre fra dig.

Det, der kendetegner dig


Du er nysgerrig, men ikke ukritisk imponeret af “AI” som buzzword
Du omsætter uklare behov til konkrete løsninger
Du lærer ved at eksperimentere og justere undervejs
Du er optaget af at bygge noget, der virker i praksis
Du samarbejder godt med både teknik og forretning



Det tilbyder vi

Du bliver en del af en AI-rejse, hvor der stadig er plads til at sætte retning og tage ansvar.

Du kommer til at arbejde med konkrete use cases, ny teknologi og løsninger, der gør en mærkbar forskel, både internt og for vores kunder.

Kort sagt:

Hvis du synes, det er sjovere at bygge noget, der virker, end at tale om, hvor smart det kunne være – så vil vi gerne høre fra dig.

Ansættelse og kontakt

Stillingen er forankret i YouSee, som en del af Consumer Division i TDC Brands.

Send din ansøgning og CV hurtigst muligt – vi afholder samtaler løbende.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte os!

Ansøgning: Det er ikke nødvendigt at vedhæfte en motiveret ansøgning, men du bedes venligst inkludere en kort introduktion i dit CV om, hvorfor du søger denne stilling. For at sikre en fair og inkluderende rekrutteringsproces beder vi dig samtidig undlade at tilføje et foto til dit CV

Ansøgningsfrist: 01/08/2026

Kontaktperson: Lasse Gilsfeldt, Head of Internal AI Products Mail: lgi@yousee.dk

Arbejdssted: Teglholmsgade 1, København & Sletvej 30, Århus

TDC Brands (Tidl. Nuuday) – Sikkert ind i fremtiden

TDC Brands består af en række stærke brands, der tilsammen leverer innovative digitale services inden for TV, bredbånd, netværk og telefoni til private kunder og virksomheder. YouSee, Telmore, Hiper og eesy servicerer danskere på tværs af alder, køn og bopæl på privatmarkedet. På erhvervsmarkedet servicerer TDC Brands mindre såvel som store erhvervsvirksomheder med TDC Erhverv og Relatel. TDC Brands beskæftiger i dag omkring 3.000 medarbejdere og har ca. 6 mio. privat- og erhvervskundeforhold. Få mere information om TDC Brands på tdcbrands.dk.

Læs mere om, hvordan vi behandler dine personoplysninger her: https://www.tdcbrands.dk/karriere

HTML:
YouSee søger AI Engineer / Fullstack Developer (Internal AI Products)

Vil du være med til at bygge AI produkter, der ikke bare lyder smarte i en PowerPoint, men faktisk skaber værdi i hverdagen?

Vi søger en AI Agentic Engineer, der vil være med til at udvikle AI-agenter og agentic workflows, som kan hjælpe forretningen med at løse konkrete opgaver smartere, hurtigere og mere ensartet.

Du behøver ikke kunne alt fra dag ét. Det vigtigste er, at du har en stærk teknisk grundforståelse, er nysgerrig og har lyst til at lære, teste, bygge og justere, når virkeligheden viser sig at være lidt mere besværlig end demoen.

Om rollen

Du bliver en central del af Internal AI Products, hvor vi udvikler og skalerer AI‑løsninger til YouSee’s ansatte med fokus på at gøre arbejdsprocesser hurtigere, nemmere og mere konsistente på tværs af systemer og funktioner.

Dit fokus er at bygge løsninger, som understøtter konkrete workflows, automatiserer opgaver og integrerer tæt med den måde medarbejdere allerede arbejder på.

Du kommer bl.a. til at arbejde med:

  • Designe og udvikle AI‑agenter samt agentic workflows
  • Bygge løsninger med LLMs, RAG, API’er og interne systemer
  • Udvikling af end‑to‑end AI‑løsninger (fra idé og prototype til produktion)
  • Integration af AI‑løsninger i eksisterende platforme og arbejdsgange
  • Arbejde med orchestration, routing, prompts og context handling på tværs af systemer
  • Tæt samarbejde med product managers, data scientists, business analysts og forretningen
  • Løbende optimering af løsninger baseret på feedback og faktisk brug i organisationen


Din profil

Vi forestiller os, at du:

  • Har erfaring med frontend og/eller backend‑udvikling og er komfortabel med at arbejde end‑to‑end
  • Har arbejdet med API‑integrationer og kan bygge løsninger, der spiller sammen med eksisterende systemer
  • Har erfaring med - eller stærk interesse for - generativ AI, LLMs eller AI‑baserede applikationer
  • Har kendskab til RAG, embeddings og/eller vector databases
  • Har erfaring med cloud‑platforme som Azure eller AWS
  • Har forståelse for, hvordan AI‑løsninger designes, bygges og bringes i produktion
  • Kan omsætte komplekse behov til konkrete, skalerbare løsninger
  • Arbejder struktureret og trives med teknisk ansvar
  • Har en nysgerrig og eksperimenterende tilgang til ny teknologi


Du behøver ikke sætte flueben ved det hele. Hvis du kan se dig selv i flere af punkterne og har lyst til at lære resten, så vil vi rigtig gerne høre fra dig.

Det, der kendetegner dig

  • Du er nysgerrig, men ikke ukritisk imponeret af “AI” som buzzword
  • Du omsætter uklare behov til konkrete løsninger
  • Du lærer ved at eksperimentere og justere undervejs
  • Du er optaget af at bygge noget, der virker i praksis
  • Du samarbejder godt med både teknik og forretning


Det tilbyder vi

Du bliver en del af en AI-rejse, hvor der stadig er plads til at sætte retning og tage ansvar.

Du kommer til at arbejde med konkrete use cases, ny teknologi og løsninger, der gør en mærkbar forskel, både internt og for vores kunder.

Kort sagt:

Hvis du synes, det er sjovere at bygge noget, der virker, end at tale om, hvor smart det kunne være – så vil vi gerne høre fra dig.

Ansættelse og kontakt

Stillingen er forankret i YouSee, som en del af Consumer Division i TDC Brands.

Send din ansøgning og CV hurtigst muligt – vi afholder samtaler løbende.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte os!

Ansøgning: Det er ikke nødvendigt at vedhæfte en motiveret ansøgning, men du bedes venligst inkludere en kort introduktion i dit CV om, hvorfor du søger denne stilling. For at sikre en fair og inkluderende rekrutteringsproces beder vi dig samtidig undlade at tilføje et foto til dit CV

Ansøgningsfrist: 01/08/2026

Kontaktperson: Lasse Gilsfeldt, Head of Internal AI Products Mail: lgi@yousee.dk

Arbejdssted: Teglholmsgade 1, København & Sletvej 30, Århus

Title: Junior Full-Stack Developer
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At NORNORM, we’re digital pioneers redefining the workspace industry through a circular business model that’s better for people, business and the planet.

As we scale, strong internal systems, data infrastructure and business insights are critical to how we operate across products, logistics, refurbishment, inventory and customer operations.

We are now looking for a Junior Full-Stack Developer to strengthen our Digital Development team and help build internal digital products, analytics capabilities and operational tools.

This is a junior role, but not an entry-level trainee position. We are looking for someone with 1–2 years of hands-on professional experience within software development, analytics engineering, backend development or similar, who is ready to contribute from early on while continuing to learn from more experienced developers.

What You'll Be Doing


Analytics backend and data platform
Maintain, improve, test and document dbt models and data transformations.
Support data pipelines in Google Cloud Platform, including BigQuery and SQL-based analytics logic.
Debug data quality issues and identify root causes across data models and pipelines.
Improve reliability, maintainability and transparency of data models, definitions and business rules.


BI platform development
Support the continued development of NORNORM’s BI tool
Contribute to backend logic, APIs, and data structures supporting BI use cases
Work with stakeholders to understand reporting and insight needs, and translate these into technical tasks together with the team
Full-stack development
Build and maintain new and existing backend services and APIs in collaboration with the development team
Support frontend work when needed
Learn and contribute to the broader NORNORM technology stack, following testing and documentation practices
Take part in communication with users, receive and take action on bug reports, feature requests, etc.

We Imagine That You Have


A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree within software engineering, computer science, IT or a related field.
1–2 years of relevant experience within software development, analytics engineering, backend development or similar.
Strong foundation in SQL and experience with Python.
Experience with software development, including Git/GitHub and writing clear, maintainable code.
Understanding of data modelling, data pipelines, APIs and backend development concepts.
Able to use AI-supported coding tools responsibly by reviewing and validating output before applying it.
Interest in growing into full-stack web development beyond the initial analytics/backend focus.
and believe that you are:
Curious, fast-learning and comfortable learning from more experienced developers.
Detail-oriented, especially when working with data logic and quality.
Pragmatic, structured and solution-oriented.
Able to communicate technical topics in a simple and understandable way.


Why join NORNORM?

If you join NORNORM, you become part of a company where technology is central to enabling and scaling a circular business model across products, logistics, refurbishment and customer operations.

You’ll get the opportunity to work on systems and digital products that play an important role in how NORNORM operates and scales as a circular business. You’ll help shape how internal tools, analytics capabilities and digital products evolve across the organisation while working closely with experienced full-stack developers and gradually taking on more ownership over time.

We offer a collaborative, high-trust environment with short decision paths, strong learning opportunities and a culture shaped by curiosity, courage and a shared ambition to help build a more circular future.

Practicalities


This role is office-first hybrid-remote: We value both flexibility and collaboration, and our rule of thumb is simple: more on-site than off-site.
Please note that we unfortunately do not offer relocation support or visa sponsorship. An existing permission to work in Denmark is therefore a mandatory requirement.


Please note that due to summer holiday, we won't start screening applications until August.

Title: Staff Analytics Engineer (1 Year Fixed-Term Contract)
Place: Odense, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
SHAPE THE FUTURE OF PRIVACY WITH USERCENTRICS



Hybrid position - 2/3 days in the office

1 year fixed-term contract


Usercentrics is a global leader in the field of consent management platforms (CMP). We follow our company vision to build a world where user privacy enables a thriving digital ecosystem. Our mission is to provide privacy solutions that empower organizations to embrace data privacy while building trust with their users through freedom of choice. With our product, businesses can collect, manage and document user consents on websites and apps, and achieve full compliance in accordance with global privacy regulations while facilitating high consent rates and building trust with their customers.

We're looking for an experienced Staff Analytics Engineer to own the design and delivery of scalable data models and data products across our data warehouse, semantic layer, and reporting layer. Reporting to the Director of Analytics, you will enable critical decision making across the business, from marketing and product to commercial operations.

This is a senior individual contributor role with technical leadership responsibilities. You will not manage a team initially, but you will set standards and mentor others. This role may evolve into a people management position as the team scales.

Your Tasks


Drive the development and expansion of our Data Warehouse and BI solutions, with a focus on simplicity, scalability, and reliability, ensuring they grow sustainably
Own and evolve the Analytics Engineering strategy, including architectural decisions, modeling standards, and the long term roadmap.
Collaborate with Data Analysts, Data Scientists, and backend engineers to deliver trusted, well modeled, analysis-ready datasets.
Apply software engineering best practices across the analytics codebase, including version control, code reviews, automated testing, lineage, and continuous integration.
Evaluate, select, and deploy tools and frameworks to strengthen data quality, governance, and reliability.
Partner closely with business stakeholders to design and scale the semantic layer to support diverse and evolving business needs, enabling and accelerating self service analytics.
Act as a go to person for Data Analysts and Data Scientists, providing guidance to improve pipelines and remove inefficiencies, redundancies, and technical bottlenecks.
Mentor and support other team members as the team scales, contributing to capability building, onboarding, and continuous knowledge sharing.
Bring strategic thinking and innovative ideas to help shape a growing team and deliver meaningful business impact at scale.


Requirements


5+ years of relevant experience in a comparable role, designing and scaling complex data models for analytics and data science. 
A unique combination of technical expertise, business acumen, and communication skills, enabling you to persuade and influence data product stakeholders. 
Proficiency with dimensional modeling (star schema) and medallion architecture, with the ability to design and develop curated data marts. 
Strong coding skills in SQL and proficiency in DBT, orchestration tools, and engineering best practices. 
Experience with cloud data warehouses (BigQuery preferred) and semantic layers such as MetricFlow, LookML, Omni, or Cube
Curiosity, strong problem solving skills, and a proactive, creative mindset.
Excellent organizational and communication skills, both verbal and written. 

Don't meet every single requirement? Studies have shown that women and people of colour are less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single qualification. At Usercentrics we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you're excited about this role but your past experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.



Benefits

Why join Usercentrics?



Joining Usercentrics means becoming part of a fast-growing, diverse and international team of tech enthusiasts and entrepreneurially-minded who build our success story together
Company culture is important to us - we strive to continuously develop a positive, vibrant and inspiring environment that enables everyone to thrive both personally and professionally
Get involved! We have plenty of initiatives and love to see people from all department enthusiastically participating and shaping our future together in different cross-department projects
Your work-life balance is important to us too - we offer flexible working hours, hybrid working and the possibility of workcations (in accordance with our company policy)
We always remember to have fun along the way, both in our day-to-day work and at our regular team events on site in our offices in Munich, Copenhagen, Odense, Lisbon and Prague or online

Title: Ai Data Scientist
Description:
A leading bank is looking for an AI Data Scientist to join their team on a long-term contract basis. You will provide deep data science expertise across Generative AI, AI-enabled tools, and applied analytics; identifying where GenAI and AI tools are most influential across coding, knowledge work, engineering workflows, SDLC flow, deployment, and DORA-style measures

Must have good experience with GenAI technologies, ideally coding assistants like GitHub Copilot or Claude
Strong understanding of SDLD, Deployment and DORA metrics and their relevance to developer productivity

Description:
Job Title: Data Scientist/Machine Learning Engineer

Location: UK

Experience: 2–5 years

Education: Master's in Science (IT/Computer Science/Engineer)

Employment Type: Full-Time

UK based candidates only.

Information Tech Consultants Ltd (ITC) is an IT management and consulting firm based in Central London. Information Tech Consultants Ltd (ITC) is an IT management and consulting firm based in Central London. We are responsible for sourcing and hiring field experts for various companies. We primarily work with Software and Cloud Hosted Services organizations as well as Mobile Apps and Service companies.

2-4 years of experience in Data science/Machine learning models from coding to deployment.
Work with datasets using Python or R, SQL, No SQL.
Assist in developing predictive and statistical models
Write and optimize queries using SQL.
Familiarity with data manipulation libraries (Pandas, NumPy), Understanding of statistics and probability.
Hands-on experience with ML libraries like Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, or PyTorch.

Hands on experience in coding (mandatory python and SQL or C++).
Understanding of Generative AI, Computer Vision cloud technology, NLP, Deep Learning.
Familiarity working with large data sets.
Project Management background preferred.

You should be entitled to work in the UK with legal work authorization status.
Must be willing to travel within the UK as per project/client requirements.
Excellent communication skills and teamwork skills.

Title: Data Scientist | Remote
Description:
Position: Member of Technical Staff (Frontier AI)

Type: Full time

Compensation: $600K - $2M/yr

Location: Remote

Role Responsibilities


Own research and evaluation initiatives end-to-end, including problem framing, data design, quality calibration, and signal validation.
Design machine learning-oriented data systems, including task definitions, annotation schemas, rubrics, incentives, and pipelines optimized for downstream model performance.
Analyze model and system failures to identify root causes, edge cases, and opportunities for improvement.
Translate ambiguous, real-world behavior into structured evaluation frameworks and new data categories.
Iterate rapidly on evaluations, datasets, and feedback loops to improve system performance.
Partner with cross-functional teams to translate research progress into clear, credible narratives grounded in evidence.


Requirements


Have strong judgment around research signal quality and when work is ready to be externalized.
Have experience designing machine learning-oriented datasets, evaluation frameworks, and quality assurance processes.
Possess the ability to translate messy, real-world system behavior into structured research and evaluation opportunities.
Be comfortable operating in ambiguity, with a bias toward ownership and decisive action.
Have clear written and verbal communication skills, especially when explaining tradeoffs and limitations to stakeholders.


Application Process


Easy Apply on LinkedIn
Check email for next steps
Participate in resume evaluation & interview stage
